   700→/// Check if a URL is allowed based on the allowed domains list.
   701→/// Returns true if allowed, false if blocked.
   702→pub fn is_domain_allowed(url: &str, allowed_domains: &[String]) -> bool {
   703→    if allowed_domains.is_empty() {
   704→        return true; // No restrictions
   705→    }
   706→
   707→    let Ok(parsed) = url::Url::parse(url) else {
   708→        return false; // Invalid URL, block it
   709→    };
   710→
   711→    let Some(host) = parsed.host_str() else {
   712→        return false; // No host, block it
   713→    };
   714→
   715→    for pattern in allowed_domains {
   716→        if pattern.starts_with("*.") {
   717→            // Wildcard: *.example.com matches foo.example.com, bar.example.com
   718→            let suffix = &pattern[1..]; // .example.com
   719→            if host.ends_with(suffix) || host == &pattern[2..] {
   720→                return true;
   721→            }
   722→        } else if host == pattern {
   723→            return true;
   724→        }
   725→    }
   726→
   727→    false
   728→}
   729→
   730→#[cfg(test)]
   731→mod tests {
   732→    use super::*;
   733→
   734→    #[test]
   735→    fn test_domain_allowed_empty_list() {
   736→        // Empty allowed_domains means all domains are allowed
   737→        assert!(is_domain_allowed("https://example.com", &[]));
   738→        assert!(is_domain_allowed("https://evil.com", &[]));
   739→    }
   740→
   741→    #[test]
   742→    fn test_domain_allowed_exact_match() {
   743→        let allowed = vec!["example.com".to_string()];
   744→        assert!(is_domain_allowed("https://example.com/path", &allowed));
   745→        assert!(!is_domain_allowed("https://other.com", &allowed));
   746→        assert!(!is_domain_allowed("https://sub.example.com", &allowed));
   747→    }
   748→
   749→    #[test]
   750→    fn test_domain_allowed_wildcard() {
   751→        let allowed = vec!["*.example.com".to_string()];
   752→        assert!(is_domain_allowed("https://sub.example.com", &allowed));
   753→        assert!(is_domain_allowed("https://foo.bar.example.com", &allowed));
   754→        // Wildcard also matches the base domain
   755→        assert!(is_domain_allowed("https://example.com", &allowed));
   756→        assert!(!is_domain_allowed("https://notexample.com", &allowed));
   757→    }
   758→
   759→    #[test]
   760→    fn test_domain_allowed_multiple() {
   761→        let allowed = vec!["example.com".to_string(), "*.trusted.org".to_string()];
   762→        assert!(is_domain_allowed("https://example.com", &allowed));
   763→        assert!(is_domain_allowed("https://sub.trusted.org", &allowed));
   764→        assert!(!is_domain_allowed("https://evil.com", &allowed));
   765→    }
   766→
   767→    #[test]
   768→    fn test_domain_allowed_invalid_url() {
   769→        let allowed = vec!["example.com".to_string()];
   770→        assert!(!is_domain_allowed("not-a-url", &allowed));
   771→        assert!(!is_domain_allowed("", &allowed));
   772→    }
   773→
   774→    #[test]
   775→    fn test_browser_preference_default_is_auto() {
   776→        assert_eq!(BrowserPreference::default(), BrowserPreference::Auto);
   777→    }
   778→
   779→    #[test]
   780→    fn test_browser_preference_deserialize() {
   781→        let value: BrowserPreference = match serde_json::from_str("\"brave\"") {
   782→            Ok(value) => value,
   783→            Err(error) => panic!("failed to deserialize browser preference: {error}"),
   784→        };
   785→        assert_eq!(value, BrowserPreference::Brave);
   786→    }
   787→
   788→    #[test]
   789→    fn resolved_profile_dir_returns_path_by_default() {
   790→        // Default config has persist_profile = true
   791→        let config = BrowserConfig::default();
   792→        let dir = config.resolved_profile_dir();
   793→        assert!(dir.is_some());
   794→        let path = dir.unwrap_or_default();
   795→        assert!(path.ends_with("browser/profile"));
   796→    }
   797→
   798→    #[test]
   799→    fn resolved_profile_dir_returns_none_when_disabled() {
   800→        let config = BrowserConfig {
   801→            persist_profile: false,
   802→            ..BrowserConfig::default()
   803→        };
   804→        assert!(config.resolved_profile_dir().is_none());
   805→    }
   806→
   807→    #[test]
   808→    fn resolved_profile_dir_uses_custom_path() {
   809→        let config = BrowserConfig {
   810→            profile_dir: Some("/custom/path".to_string()),
   811→            ..BrowserConfig::default()
   812→        };
   813→        let dir = config.resolved_profile_dir();
   814→        assert_eq!(dir, Some(std::path::PathBuf::from("/custom/path")));
   815→    }
   816→
   817→    #[test]
   818→    fn resolved_profile_dir_custom_overrides_persist_flag() {
   819→        let config = BrowserConfig {
   820→            persist_profile: false,
   821→            profile_dir: Some("/override".to_string()),
   822→            ..BrowserConfig::default()
   823→        };
   824→        // profile_dir takes precedence, implicitly enabling persistence
   825→        assert!(config.resolved_profile_dir().is_some());
   826→    }
   827→
   828→    #[test]
   829→    fn browser_request_deserializes_after_null_stripping() {
   830→        // LLMs often send explicit nulls for optional/defaulted fields.
   831→        // The tool strips nulls before deserializing, so simulate that here.
   832→        let raw = serde_json::json!({
   833→            "action": "navigate",
   834→            "url": "https://example.com",
   835→            "session_id": null,
   836→            "browser": null,
   837→            "timeout_ms": null,
   838→            "sandbox": null,
   839→        });
   840→
   841→        // Without stripping, this would fail: "invalid type: null, expected u64"
   842→        let mut obj = raw.as_object().unwrap_or(&serde_json::Map::new()).clone();
   843→        obj.retain(|_, v| !v.is_null());
   844→        let cleaned = serde_json::Value::Object(obj);
   845→
   846→        let req: BrowserRequest = serde_json::from_value(cleaned)
   847→            .expect("should deserialize after null-stripping");
   848→        assert!(req.session_id.is_none());
   849→        assert_eq!(req.timeout_ms, 60000); // default
   850→        assert!(req.browser.is_none());
   851→        assert!(req.sandbox.is_none());
   852→        matches!(req.action, BrowserAction::Navigate { .. });
   853→    }
   854→}
   855→

1→//! Default configuration template with all options documented.
     2→//!
     3→//! This template is used when creating a new config file. It includes all
     4→//! available options with descriptions, allowing users to see everything
     5→//! that can be configured even if they don't change the defaults.
     6→
     7→/// Generate the default config template with a specific port.
     8→pub fn default_config_template(port: u16) -> String {
     9→    format!(
    10→        r##"# Moltis Configuration
    11→# ====================
    12→# This file contains all available configuration options.
    13→# Uncomment and modify settings as needed.
    14→# Changes require a restart to take effect.
    15→#
    16→# Environment variable substitution is supported: ${{ENV_VAR}}
    17→# Example: api_key = "${{ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}}"
    18→
    19→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    20→# SERVER
    21→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    22→
    23→[server]
    24→bind = "127.0.0.1"                # Address to bind to ("0.0.0.0" for all interfaces)
    25→port = {port}                           # Port number (auto-generated for this installation)
    26→http_request_logs = false              # Enable verbose Axum HTTP request/response logs (debugging)
    27→ws_request_logs = false                # Enable WebSocket RPC request/response logs (debugging)
    28→update_releases_url = "https://www.moltis.org/releases.json"    # Releases manifest URL for update checks (override to use a custom URL)
    29→
    30→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    31→# AUTHENTICATION
    32→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    33→
    34→[auth]
    35→disabled = false                  # true = disable auth entirely (DANGEROUS if exposed)
    36→                                  # When disabled, anyone with network access can use moltis
    37→
    38→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    39→# GRAPHQL
    40→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    41→
    42→[graphql]
    43→enabled = false                   # Enable GraphQL endpoint (/graphql for HTTP + WebSocket)
    44→                                  # Can be toggled at runtime in Settings > GraphQL
    45→
    46→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    47→# TLS / HTTPS
    48→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    49→
    50→[tls]
    51→enabled = true                    # Enable HTTPS (recommended)
    52→auto_generate = true              # Auto-generate local CA and server certificate
    53→# http_redirect_port = 18790      # Optional override (default: server.port + 1)
    54→# cert_path = "/path/to/cert.pem"     # Custom certificate file (overrides auto-gen)
    55→# key_path = "/path/to/key.pem"       # Custom private key file
    56→# ca_cert_path = "/path/to/ca.pem"    # CA certificate for trust instructions
    57→
    58→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    59→# AGENT IDENTITY
    60→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    61→# Customize your agent's personality. These are typically set during onboarding.
    62→
    63→[identity]
    64→# name = "moltis"                 # Agent's display name
    65→# emoji = "🦊"                    # Agent's emoji/avatar
    66→# theme = "wise owl"              # Theme for agent personality (e.g. wise owl, chill fox)
    67→# soul = ""                       # Freeform personality text injected into system prompt
    68→                                  # Use this for custom instructions, tone, or behavior
    69→
    70→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    71→# USER PROFILE
    72→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    73→# Information about you. Set during onboarding.
    74→
    75→[user]
    76→# name = "Your Name"              # Your name (used in conversations)
    77→# timezone = "America/New_York"   # Your timezone (IANA format)
    78→
    79→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    80→# LLM PROVIDERS
    81→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    82→# Configure API keys and settings for each LLM provider.
    83→# API keys can also be set via environment variables (preferred for security).
    84→#
    85→# Each provider supports:
    86→#   enabled   - Whether to use this provider (default: true)
    87→#   api_key   - API key (or use env var like ANTHROPIC_API_KEY)
    88→#   base_url  - Override API endpoint
    89→#   models    - Preferred models shown first (optional)
    90→#   fetch_models - Discover models from provider API when available (default: true)
    91→#   stream_transport - Streaming transport: "sse", "websocket", or "auto" (default: "sse")
    92→#   alias     - Custom name for metrics labels (useful for multiple instances)
    93→
    94→[providers]
    95→offered = ["local-llm", "github-copilot", "openai-codex", "openai", "anthropic", "openrouter", "ollama", "moonshot", "minimax", "zai"] # Enabled providers and those shown in onboarding/picker UI ([] = enable/show all)
    96→# All available providers:
    97→#   "anthropic", "openai", "gemini", "groq", "xai", "deepseek",
    98→#   "fireworks", "mistral", "openrouter", "cerebras", "minimax",
    99→#   "moonshot", "zai", "zai-code", "venice", "ollama", "local-llm", "openai-codex",
   100→#   "github-copilot", "kimi-code"
   101→
   102→# ── Anthropic (Claude) ────────────────────────────────────────
   103→# [providers.anthropic]
   104→# enabled = true
   105→# api_key = "sk-ant-..."                      # Or set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var
   106→# models = ["claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929"]     # Optional preferred models
   107→# fetch_models = true                          # Set false to skip remote discovery
   108→# base_url = "https://api.anthropic.com"     # API endpoint
   109→# alias = "anthropic"                         # Custom name for metrics
   110→# cache_retention = "short"                    # Prompt caching: "none" | "short" | "long"
   111→
   112→# ── OpenAI ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   113→[providers.openai]
   114→# enabled = true
   115→# api_key = "sk-..."                          # Or set OPENAI_API_KEY env var
   116→models = ["gpt-5.3", "gpt-5.2"]              # Preferred models shown first
   117→# fetch_models = true
   118→# stream_transport = "sse"                     # "sse" | "websocket" | "auto"
   119→# base_url = "https://api.openai.com/v1"     # API endpoint (change for Azure, etc.)
   120→# alias = "openai"
   121→
   122→# ── Google Gemini ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   123→# [providers.gemini]
   124→# enabled = true
   125→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set GEMINI_API_KEY / GOOGLE_API_KEY env var
   126→# models = ["gemini-2.5-flash-preview-05-20", "gemini-2.0-flash"]
   127→# fetch_models = true
   128→# base_url = "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/openai"
   129→# alias = "gemini"
   130→
   131→# ── Groq ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   132→# [providers.groq]
   133→# enabled = true
   134→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set GROQ_API_KEY env var
   135→# models = ["llama-3.3-70b-versatile"]
   136→# alias = "groq"
   137→
   138→# ── DeepSeek ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
   139→# [providers.deepseek]
   140→# enabled = true
   141→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set DEEPSEEK_API_KEY env var
   142→# models = ["deepseek-chat"]
   143→# base_url = "https://api.deepseek.com"
   144→# alias = "deepseek"
   145→
   146→# ── Fireworks ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   147→# [providers.fireworks]
   148→# enabled = true
   149→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set FIREWORKS_API_KEY env var
   150→# models = ["accounts/fireworks/routers/kimi-k2p5-turbo"]
   151→# fetch_models = true                          # Set false to skip remote discovery
   152→# base_url = "https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1"
   153→# alias = "fireworks"
   154→
   155→# ── xAI (Grok) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   156→# [providers.xai]
   157→# enabled = true
   158→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set XAI_API_KEY env var
   159→# models = ["grok-3-mini"]
   160→# alias = "xai"
   161→
   162→# ── OpenRouter (multi-provider gateway) ───────────────────────
   163→# [providers.openrouter]
   164→# enabled = true
   165→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var
   166→# models = ["anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet"]    # Any model IDs on OpenRouter
   167→# base_url = "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1"
   168→
   169→# ── Moonshot (Kimi) ─────────────────────────────────────────
   170→[providers.moonshot]
   171→# enabled = true
   172→# api_key = "..."                             # Or set MOONSHOT_API_KEY env var
   173→models = ["kimi-k2.5"]                        # Preferred models shown first
   174→# base_url = "https://api.moonshot.ai/v1"
   175→# alias = "moonshot"
   176→
   177→[providers.ollama]
   178→# base_url = "http://localhost:11434"
   179→# models = ["llama3.2", "qwen2.5:7b"]         # Optional preferred models; installed models are discovered dynamically
   180→
   181→[providers.local-llm]
   182→# models = ["qwen2.5-coder-7b-q4_k_m"]        # Optional; configure local models in onboarding
   183→
   184→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   185→# CHAT SETTINGS
   186→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   187→
   188→[chat]
   189→message_queue_mode = "followup"   # Default: process queued messages one-by-one after the current run.
   190→                                  # How to handle messages during an active agent run:
   191→                                  #   "followup" - Queue messages, replay one-by-one after run
   192→                                  #   "collect"  - Buffer messages, concatenate as single message
   193→# priority_models = ["claude-opus-4-5", "gpt-5.2", "gemini-3-flash"]  # Optional: models to pin first in selectors
   194→# allowed_models = ["gpt 5.2"]  # Legacy field (currently ignored).
   195→
   196→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   197→# SPAWN PRESETS (OPTIONAL)
   198→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   199→# Configure reusable presets for the `spawn_agent` tool.
   200→#
   201→# [agents]
   202→# default_preset = "research"      # Optional: used when spawn_agent.preset is omitted
   203→#
   204→# [agents.presets.research]
   205→# model = "openai/gpt-5.2"
   206→# allow_tools = ["web_search", "web_fetch", "sessions_send", "task_list"]
   207→# deny_tools = ["exec"]
   208→# delegate_only = false
   209→# system_prompt_suffix = "Focus on gathering and summarizing evidence."
   210→
   211→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   212→# TOOLS
   213→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   214→
   215→[tools]
   216→agent_timeout_secs = 600          # Max seconds for an agent run (0 = no timeout)
   217→agent_max_iterations = 25         # Max LLM/tool loop iterations before stopping
   218→max_tool_result_bytes = 50000     # Max bytes per tool result before truncation (50KB)
   219→# registry_mode = "full"          # "full" = all schemas every turn, "lazy" = tool_search discovery
   220→
   221→# ── Maps ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   222→
   223→[tools.maps]
   224→provider = "google_maps"          # Map provider used by show_map:
   225→                                  #   "google_maps" (default)
   226→                                  #   "apple_maps"
   227→                                  #   "openstreetmap"
   228→
   229→# ── Command Execution ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   230→
   231→[tools.exec]
   232→default_timeout_secs = 30         # Default timeout for commands
   233→max_output_bytes = 204800         # Max command output bytes (200KB)
   234→approval_mode = "on-miss"         # When to require approval:
   235→                                  #   "always"  - Always ask before running
   236→                                  #   "on-miss" - Ask if not in allowlist
   237→                                  #   "never"   - Never ask (dangerous)
   238→security_level = "allowlist"      # Security mode:
   239→                                  #   "permissive" - Allow most commands
   240→                                  #   "allowlist"  - Only allow listed commands
   241→                                  #   "strict"     - Very restrictive
   242→allowlist = []                    # Command patterns to allow (when security_level = "allowlist")
   243→                                  # Example: ["git *", "npm *", "cargo *"]
   244→host = "local"                    # Where to run commands:
   245→                                  #   "local" - Run on this machine (default)
   246→                                  #   "node"  - Run on a connected Moltis node
   247→                                  #   "ssh"   - Run through the system ssh client
   248→# node = "mac-mini"               # Default node id/display name when host = "node"
   249→# ssh_target = "deploy@box"       # SSH host alias or user@host when host = "ssh"
   250→
   251→# ── Sandbox Configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   252→# Commands run inside isolated containers for security.
   253→
   254→[tools.exec.sandbox]
   255→mode = "all"                      # Which commands to sandbox:
   256→                                  #   "off"      - No sandboxing (commands run on host)
   257→                                  #   "non-main" - Sandbox all except main session
   258→                                  #   "all"      - Sandbox everything (recommended)
   259→scope = "session"                 # Container lifecycle:
   260→                                  #   "command" - New container per command
   261→                                  #   "session" - Container per session (recommended)
   262→                                  #   "global"  - Single shared container
   263→workspace_mount = "ro"            # How to mount workspace in sandbox:
   264→                                  #   "ro"   - Read-only (safe)
   265→                                  #   "rw"   - Read-write (can modify files)
   266→                                  #   "none" - No mount
   267→# host_data_dir = "/host/path/data"  # Optional override if auto-detection cannot resolve the host-visible data dir
   268→home_persistence = "shared"       # Persist /home/sandbox across container recreation:
   269→                                  #   "off"     - Ephemeral home
   270→                                  #   "session" - Per-session persisted home
   271→                                  #   "shared"  - One shared persisted home (default)
   272→# shared_home_dir = "/path/to/shared-home"  # Host dir for shared persistence (default: data_dir()/sandbox/home/shared)
   273→backend = "auto"                  # Container backend:
   274→                                  #   "auto"            - Auto-detect (prefers Apple Container on macOS)
   275→                                  #   "docker"          - Use Docker
   276→                                  #   "apple-container" - Use Apple Container (macOS only)
   277→no_network = true                 # Disable network access in sandbox (recommended)
   278→# image = "custom-image:tag"      # Custom Docker image (default: auto-built)
   279→# container_prefix = "moltis"     # Prefix for container names
   280→
   281→# Packages installed in sandbox containers via apt-get.
   282→# This list is used to build the sandbox image. Customize as needed.
   283→packages = [
   284→    # Networking & HTTP
   285→    "curl",
   286→    "wget",
   287→    "ca-certificates",
   288→    "dnsutils",
   289→    "netcat-openbsd",
   290→    "openssh-client",
   291→    "iproute2",
   292→    "net-tools",
   293→    # Language runtimes
   294→    "python3",
   295→    "python3-dev",
   296→    "python3-pip",
   297→    "python3-venv",
   298→    "python-is-python3",
   299→    "nodejs",
   300→    "npm",
   301→    "ruby",
   302→    "ruby-dev",
   303→    "golang-go",
   304→    # Build toolchain & native deps
   305→    "build-essential",
   306→    "clang",
   307→    "libclang-dev",
   308→    "llvm-dev",
   309→    "pkg-config",
   310→    "libssl-dev",
   311→    "libsqlite3-dev",
   312→    "libyaml-dev",
   313→    "liblzma-dev",
   314→    "autoconf",
   315→    "automake",
   316→    "libtool",
   317→    "bison",
   318→    "flex",
   319→    "dpkg-dev",
   320→    "fakeroot",
   321→    # Compression & archiving
   322→    "zip",
   323→    "unzip",
   324→    "bzip2",
   325→    "xz-utils",
   326→    "p7zip-full",
   327→    "tar",
   328→    "zstd",
   329→    "lz4",
   330→    "pigz",
   331→    # Common CLI utilities
   332→    "git",
   333→    "gnupg2",
   334→    "jq",
   335→    "rsync",
   336→    "file",
   337→    "tree",
   338→    "sqlite3",
   339→    "sudo",
   340→    "locales",
   341→    "tzdata",
   342→    "shellcheck",
   343→    "patchelf",
   344→    "tmux",
   345→    # Text processing & search
   346→    "ripgrep",
   347→    # Browser automation dependencies
   348→    "chromium",
   349→    "libxss1",
   350→    "libnss3",
   351→    "libnspr4",
   352→    "libasound2t64",
   353→    "libatk1.0-0t64",
   354→    "libatk-bridge2.0-0t64",
   355→    "libcups2t64",
   356→    "libdrm2",
   357→    "libgbm1",
   358→    "libgtk-3-0t64",
   359→    "libxcomposite1",
   360→    "libxdamage1",
   361→    "libxfixes3",
   362→    "libxrandr2",
   363→    "libxkbcommon0",
   364→    "fonts-liberation",
   365→]
   366→
   367→# Resource limits for sandboxed execution (optional)
   368→[tools.exec.sandbox.resource_limits]
   369→# memory_limit = "512M"           # Memory limit (e.g., "512M", "1G", "2G")
   370→# cpu_quota = 0.5                 # CPU quota as fraction (0.5 = half a core, 2.0 = two cores)
   371→# pids_max = 100                  # Maximum number of processes
   372→
   373→# ── Tool Policy ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   374→# Control which tools agents can use.
   375→
   376→[tools.policy]
   377→allow = []                        # Tools to always allow (e.g., ["exec", "web_fetch"])
   378→deny = []                         # Tools to always deny (e.g., ["browser"])
   379→# profile = "default"             # Named policy profile
   380→
   381→# ── Web Search ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   382→
   383→[tools.web.search]
   384→enabled = true                    # Enable web search tool
   385→provider = "brave"                # Search provider: "brave" or "perplexity"
   386→max_results = 5                   # Number of results to return (1-10)
   387→timeout_seconds = 30              # HTTP request timeout
   388→cache_ttl_minutes = 15            # Cache results for this many minutes (0 = no cache)
   389→duckduckgo_fallback = false       # Off by default; enable only if you want DDG fallback without API keys
   390→# api_key = "..."                 # Brave API key (or set BRAVE_API_KEY env var)
   391→
   392→# Perplexity-specific settings (when provider = "perplexity")
   393→[tools.web.search.perplexity]
   394→# api_key = "..."                 # Or set PERPLEXITY_API_KEY env var
   395→# base_url = "..."                # API base URL (auto-detected from key prefix)
   396→# model = "sonar"                 # Perplexity model to use
   397→
   398→# ── Web Fetch ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   399→
   400→[tools.web.fetch]
   401→enabled = true                    # Enable web fetch tool
   402→max_chars = 50000                 # Max characters to return from fetched content
   403→timeout_seconds = 30              # HTTP request timeout
   404→cache_ttl_minutes = 15            # Cache fetched pages for this many minutes (0 = no cache)
   405→max_redirects = 3                 # Maximum HTTP redirects to follow
   406→readability = true                # Use readability extraction for HTML (cleaner output)
   407→# ssrf_allowlist = ["172.22.0.0/16"] # CIDR ranges exempt from SSRF blocking (e.g. Docker networks)
   408→
   409→# ── Browser Automation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   410→# Full browser control via Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP).
   411→# Use for JavaScript-heavy sites, form filling, screenshots.
   412→
   413→[tools.browser]
   414→enabled = true                    # Enable browser tool
   415→headless = true                   # Run without visible window (true = background)
   416→viewport_width = 2560             # Default viewport width in pixels (QHD for tech users)
   417→viewport_height = 1440            # Default viewport height in pixels
   418→device_scale_factor = 2.0         # HiDPI/Retina scaling (2.0 = Retina, 1.0 = standard)
   419→max_instances = 3                 # Maximum concurrent browser instances
   420→idle_timeout_secs = 300           # Close idle browsers after this many seconds (5 min)
   421→navigation_timeout_ms = 30000     # Page load timeout in milliseconds (30 sec)
   422→sandbox = false                   # Run browser in Docker/Apple Container for isolation
   423→# container_host = "127.0.0.1"   # Host/IP to reach browser container (default: localhost)
   424→                                  # Set to "host.docker.internal" when Moltis runs inside Docker
   425→# chrome_path = "/path/to/chrome" # Custom Chrome/Chromium binary path (auto-detected)
   426→# user_agent = "Custom UA"        # Custom user agent string
   427→# chrome_args = []                # Extra Chrome command-line arguments
   428→                                  # Example: ["--disable-extensions", "--disable-gpu"]
   429→
   430→# Domain restrictions for security.
   431→# When set, browser will refuse to navigate to domains not in this list.
   432→# This helps prevent prompt injection from untrusted websites.
   433→allowed_domains = []              # Empty = all domains allowed
   434→# allowed_domains = [
   435→#     "docs.example.com",         # Exact match
   436→#     "*.github.com",             # Wildcard: matches any subdomain of github.com
   437→#     "localhost",                # Allow localhost
   438→#     "127.0.0.1",
   439→# ]
   440→
   441→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   442→# SKILLS
   443→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   444→# Reusable prompt templates and workflows.
   445→
   446→[skills]
   447→enabled = true                    # Enable skills system
   448→search_paths = []                 # Additional directories to search for skills
   449→                                  # Default locations include ~/.moltis/skills/
   450→auto_load = []                    # Skills to always load without explicit activation
   451→                                  # Example: ["code-review", "commit"]
   452→enable_agent_sidecar_files = false # Allow agents to write supplementary text files inside personal skill dirs
   453→
   454→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   455→# MCP SERVERS
   456→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   457→# Model Context Protocol servers provide additional tools and capabilities.
   458→# See https://modelcontextprotocol.io for available servers.
   459→
   460→[mcp]
   461→request_timeout_secs = 30        # Default timeout for MCP requests
   462→# Each server has a name and configuration:
   463→#
   464→# [mcp.servers.server-name]
   465→# command = "npx"                 # Command to run (for stdio transport)
   466→# args = ["-y", "@package/name"]  # Command arguments
   467→# env = {{ KEY = "value" }}         # Environment variables for the process
   468→# enabled = true                  # Whether this server is enabled
   469→# request_timeout_secs = 90       # Optional timeout override for this server
   470→# transport = "stdio"             # Transport: "stdio" (default) or "sse"
   471→# url = "http://..."              # URL for SSE transport
   472→# headers = {{ Authorization = "Bearer ${{TOKEN}}" }}  # Optional HTTP headers for SSE transport
   473→
   474→# Example: Filesystem access
   475→# [mcp.servers.filesystem]
   476→# command = "npx"
   477→# args = ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/path/to/allow"]
   478→# enabled = true
   479→
   480→# Example: GitHub integration
   481→# [mcp.servers.github]
   482→# command = "npx"
   483→# args = ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
   484→# env = {{ GITHUB_TOKEN = "${{GITHUB_TOKEN}}" }}
   485→# enabled = true
   486→
   487→# Example: SSE server
   488→# [mcp.servers.remote]
   489→# transport = "sse"
   490→# url = "http://localhost:8080/mcp?api_key=$REMOTE_MCP_KEY"
   491→# headers = {{ "x-api-key" = "${{REMOTE_MCP_KEY}}" }}
   492→# enabled = true
   493→
   494→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   495→# METRICS
   496→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   497→# Prometheus metrics for observability.
   498→
   499→[metrics]
   500→enabled = true                    # Enable metrics collection
   501→prometheus_endpoint = true        # Expose /metrics endpoint for Prometheus scraping
   502→# labels = {{ environment = "production", instance = "main" }}
   503→                                  # Additional labels to add to all metrics
   504→
   505→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   506→# HEARTBEAT
   507→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   508→# Periodic health-check agent turns to keep the agent "alive" and responsive.
   509→
   510→[heartbeat]
   511→enabled = true                    # Enable periodic heartbeats
   512→every = "30m"                     # Interval between heartbeats (e.g., "30m", "1h", "6h")
   513→# model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-20250514"  # Override model for heartbeats
   514→# prompt = "..."                  # Custom heartbeat prompt (default: built-in)
   515→ack_max_chars = 300               # Max characters for acknowledgment reply
   516→deliver = false                   # Deliver heartbeat replies to a channel account
   517→# channel = "my-bot"              # Channel account identifier (required when deliver = true)
   518→# to = "123456789"                # Chat/recipient ID (required when deliver = true)
   519→sandbox_enabled = true            # Run heartbeat commands in sandbox
   520→# sandbox_image = "..."           # Override sandbox image for heartbeats
   521→
   522→# Active hours window - heartbeats only run during this time
   523→[heartbeat.active_hours]
   524→start = "08:00"                   # Start time (HH:MM, 24-hour format)
   525→end = "24:00"                     # End time (HH:MM, "24:00" = end of day)
   526→timezone = "local"                # Timezone: "local" or IANA name like "Europe/Paris"
   527→
   528→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   529→# FAILOVER
   530→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   531→# Automatic fallback to alternative models/providers on failure.
   532→
   533→[failover]
   534→enabled = true                    # Enable automatic failover
   535→fallback_models = []              # Ordered list of fallback models
   536→                                  # Empty = auto-build chain from all registered models
   537→                                  # Example: ["openai/gpt-4o", "anthropic/claude-3-haiku"]
   538→
   539→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   540→# VOICE
   541→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   542→# Voice provider settings for text-to-speech (TTS) and speech-to-text (STT).
   543→# `providers` controls what appears in the Settings UI provider list.
   544→
   545→[voice.tts]
   546→enabled = true                    # Enable text-to-speech
   547→# provider = "openai"             # Active TTS provider (auto-selects first configured if omitted)
   548→providers = ["openai", "elevenlabs"] # UI allowlist (empty = show all TTS providers)
   549→# All available TTS providers:
   550→#   "openai", "elevenlabs", "google", "piper", "coqui"
   551→
   552→[voice.stt]
   553→enabled = true                    # Enable speech-to-text
   554→# provider = "whisper"            # Active STT provider (auto-selects first configured if omitted)
   555→providers = ["whisper", "mistral", "elevenlabs"] # UI allowlist (empty = show all STT providers)
   556→# All available STT providers:
   557→#   "whisper", "groq", "deepgram", "google", "mistral",
   558→#   "voxtral-local", "whisper-cli", "sherpa-onnx", "elevenlabs-stt"
   559→
   560→# No api_key needed for OpenAI TTS/Whisper when OpenAI is configured as an LLM provider.
   561→# [voice.tts.openai]
   562→# voice = "alloy"                 # alloy, echo, fable, onyx, nova, shimmer
   563→# model = "tts-1"                 # tts-1 or tts-1-hd
   564→
   565→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   566→# TAILSCALE
   567→# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
   568→# Expose moltis via Tailscale Serve (private) or Funnel (public).
   569→
   570→[tailscale]
   571→mode = "off"                      # Tailscale mode:
   572→                                  #   "off"    - Disabled
   573→                                  #   "serve"  - Tailnet-only HTTPS (private)
   574→                                  #   "funnel" - Public HTTPS via Tailscale
   575→reset_on_exit = true              # Reset serve/funnel when gateway shuts down

   578→# MEMORY / EMBEDDINGS

   580→# Configure the embedding provider for memory/RAG features.

   693→#     # ── Modifying events (can block or modify payload) ──
   694→#     "BeforeAgentStart",          # Before the agent loop starts

   696→#     "AfterLLMCall",              # After LLM response, before tool execution

   700→#     "ToolResultPersist",         # When a tool result is persisted
   701→#     #
   702→#     # ── Read-only events (observe only, run in parallel) ──
   703→#     "AfterToolCall",             # After a tool completes

   705→#     "AgentEnd",                  # When the agent loop finishes
   706→#     "MessageReceived",           # When a user message arrives
   707→#     "MessageSent",               # After a response is delivered
   708→#     "SessionStart",              # When a new session begins
   709→#     "SessionEnd",                # When a session ends
   710→#     "GatewayStart",              # When Moltis starts
   711→#     "GatewayStop",               # When Moltis shuts down
   712→#     "Command",                   # When a slash command is used
   713→# ]
   714→# timeout = 10                    # Command timeout in seconds
   715→# [hooks.hooks.env]               # Environment variables passed to command

1→# Browser Automation
     2→
     3→Moltis provides full browser automation via Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP),
     4→enabling agents to interact with JavaScript-heavy websites, fill forms,
     5→click buttons, and capture screenshots.
     6→
     7→## Overview
     8→
     9→Browser automation is useful when you need to:
    10→
    11→- Interact with SPAs (Single Page Applications)
    12→- Fill forms and click buttons
    13→- Navigate sites that require JavaScript rendering
    14→- Take screenshots of pages
    15→- Execute JavaScript in page context
    16→- Maintain session state across multiple interactions
    17→
    18→For simple page content retrieval (static HTML), prefer `web_fetch` as it's
    19→faster and more lightweight.
    20→
    21→## Architecture
    22→
    23→```
    24→┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐
    25→│   BrowserTool   │────▶│  BrowserManager │────▶│   BrowserPool    │
    26→│   (AgentTool)   │     │   (actions)     │     │   (instances)    │
    27→└─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘
    28→                                                         │
    29→                                                         ▼
    30→                                                ┌──────────────────┐
    31→                                                │  Chrome/Chromium │
    32→                                                │     via CDP      │
    33→                                                └──────────────────┘
    34→```
    35→
    36→### Components
    37→
    38→- **BrowserTool** (`crates/tools/src/browser.rs`) - AgentTool wrapper for LLM
    39→- **BrowserManager** (`crates/browser/src/manager.rs`) - High-level action API
    40→- **BrowserPool** (`crates/browser/src/pool.rs`) - Chrome instance management
    41→- **Snapshot** (`crates/browser/src/snapshot.rs`) - DOM element extraction
    42→
    43→## Configuration
    44→
    45→Browser automation is **enabled by default**. To customize, add to your `moltis.toml`:
    46→
    47→```toml
    48→[tools.browser]
    49→enabled = true              # Enable browser support
    50→headless = true             # Run without visible window (default)
    51→viewport_width = 2560       # Default viewport width
    52→viewport_height = 1440      # Default viewport height
    53→device_scale_factor = 2.0   # HiDPI/Retina scaling (1.0 = standard, 2.0 = Retina)
    54→
    55→# Pool management
    56→max_instances = 0           # 0 = unlimited (limited by memory), >0 = hard limit
    57→memory_limit_percent = 90   # Block new instances when memory exceeds this %
    58→idle_timeout_secs = 300     # Close idle browsers after 5 min
    59→navigation_timeout_ms = 30000  # Page load timeout
    60→
    61→# Optional customization
    62→# chrome_path = "/path/to/chrome"  # Custom Chrome path
    63→# user_agent = "Custom UA"         # Custom user agent
    64→# chrome_args = ["--disable-extensions"]  # Extra args
    65→
    66→# Sandbox image (browser sandbox mode follows session sandbox mode)
    67→sandbox_image = "browserless/chrome"  # Container image for sandboxed sessions
    68→# allowed_domains = ["example.com", "*.trusted.org"]  # Restrict navigation
    69→
    70→# Container connectivity (for Moltis-in-Docker setups)
    71→# container_host = "127.0.0.1"  # Default; change when Moltis runs inside Docker
    72→```
    73→
    74→### Memory-Based Pool Limits
    75→
    76→By default, browser instances are limited by system memory rather than a fixed count:
    77→
    78→- `max_instances = 0` (default): Unlimited instances, blocked only when memory
    79→  exceeds `memory_limit_percent`
    80→- `memory_limit_percent = 90`: New browsers blocked when system memory > 90%
    81→- Set `max_instances > 0` for a hard limit if you prefer fixed constraints
    82→
    83→This allows multiple chat sessions to each have their own browser without
    84→artificial limits, while protecting system stability when memory is constrained.
    85→
    86→### Domain Restrictions
    87→
    88→For improved security, you can restrict which domains the browser can navigate to:
    89→
    90→```toml
    91→[tools.browser]
    92→allowed_domains = [
    93→    "docs.example.com",    # Exact match
    94→    "*.github.com",        # Wildcard: matches any subdomain
    95→    "localhost",           # Allow localhost
    96→]
    97→```
    98→
    99→When `allowed_domains` is set, any navigation to a domain not in the list will
   100→be blocked with an error. Wildcards (`*.domain.com`) match any subdomain and
   101→also the base domain itself.
   102→
   103→## Tool Usage
   104→
   105→### Actions
   106→
   107→| Action | Description | Required Params |
   108→|--------|-------------|-----------------|
   109→| `navigate` | Go to a URL | `url` |
   110→| `snapshot` | Get DOM with element refs | - |
   111→| `screenshot` | Capture page image | `full_page` (optional) |
   112→| `click` | Click element by ref | `ref_` |
   113→| `type` | Type into element | `ref_`, `text` |
   114→| `scroll` | Scroll page/element | `x`, `y`, `ref_` (optional) |
   115→| `evaluate` | Run JavaScript | `code` |
   116→| `wait` | Wait for element | `selector` or `ref_` |
   117→| `get_url` | Get current URL | - |
   118→| `get_title` | Get page title | - |
   119→| `back` | Go back in history | - |
   120→| `forward` | Go forward in history | - |
   121→| `refresh` | Reload the page | - |
   122→| `close` | Close browser session | - |
   123→
   124→### Automatic Session Tracking
   125→
   126→The browser tool automatically tracks and reuses session IDs. After a `navigate`
   127→action creates a session, subsequent actions will reuse it without needing to
   128→pass `session_id` explicitly:
   129→
   130→```json
   131→// 1. Navigate (creates session)
   132→{ "action": "navigate", "url": "https://example.com" }
   133→
   134→// 2. Snapshot (session_id auto-injected)
   135→{ "action": "snapshot" }
   136→
   137→// 3. Click (session_id auto-injected)
   138→{ "action": "click", "ref_": 1 }
   139→
   140→// 4. Screenshot (session_id auto-injected)
   141→{ "action": "screenshot" }
   142→
   143→// 5. Close (clears tracked session)
   144→{ "action": "close" }
   145→```
   146→
   147→This prevents pool exhaustion from LLMs that forget to pass the session_id.
   148→
   149→### Browser selection
   150→
   151→You can ask for a specific browser at runtime (host mode):
   152→
   153→```json
   154→{ "action": "navigate", "url": "https://example.com", "browser": "brave" }
   155→```
   156→
   157→Supported values: `auto`, `chrome`, `chromium`, `edge`, `brave`, `opera`,
   158→`vivaldi`, `arc`.
   159→
   160→`auto` (default) picks the first detected installed browser. If none are
   161→installed, Moltis will attempt a best-effort auto-install, then retry
   162→detection.
   163→
   164→### Workflow Example
   165→
   166→```json
   167→// 1. Navigate to a page
   168→{
   169→  "action": "navigate",
   170→  "url": "https://example.com/login"
   171→}
   172→// Returns: { "session_id": "browser-abc123", "url": "https://..." }
   173→
   174→// 2. Get interactive elements (session_id optional - auto-tracked)
   175→{
   176→  "action": "snapshot"
   177→}
   178→// Returns element refs like:
   179→// { "elements": [
   180→//   { "ref_": 1, "tag": "input", "role": "textbox", "placeholder": "Email" },
   181→//   { "ref_": 2, "tag": "input", "role": "textbox", "placeholder": "Password" },
   182→//   { "ref_": 3, "tag": "button", "role": "button", "text": "Sign In" }
   183→// ]}
   184→
   185→// 3. Fill in the form
   186→{
   187→  "action": "type",
   188→  "ref_": 1,
   189→  "text": "user@example.com"
   190→}
   191→
   192→{
   193→  "action": "type",
   194→  "ref_": 2,
   195→  "text": "password123"
   196→}
   197→
   198→// 4. Click the submit button
   199→{
   200→  "action": "click",
   201→  "ref_": 3
   202→}
   203→
   204→// 5. Take a screenshot of the result
   205→{
   206→  "action": "screenshot"
   207→}
   208→// Returns: { "screenshot": "data:image/png;base64,..." }
   209→```
   210→
   211→## Element Reference System
   212→
   213→The snapshot action extracts interactive elements and assigns them numeric
   214→references. This approach (inspired by [OpenClaw](https://docs.openclaw.ai))
   215→provides:
   216→
   217→- **Stability**: References don't break with minor page updates
   218→- **Security**: No CSS selectors exposed to the model
   219→- **Reliability**: Elements identified by role/content, not fragile paths
   220→
   221→### Extracted Element Info
   222→
   223→```json
   224→{
   225→  "ref_": 1,
   226→  "tag": "button",
   227→  "role": "button",
   228→  "text": "Submit",
   229→  "href": null,
   230→  "placeholder": null,
   231→  "value": null,
   232→  "aria_label": "Submit form",
   233→  "visible": true,
   234→  "interactive": true,
   235→  "bounds": { "x": 100, "y": 200, "width": 80, "height": 40 }
   236→}
   237→```
   238→
   239→## Comparison: Browser vs Web Fetch
   240→
   241→| Feature | `web_fetch` | `browser` |
   242→|---------|-------------|-----------|
   243→| Speed | Fast | Slower |
   244→| Resources | Minimal | Chrome instance |
   245→| JavaScript | No | Yes |
   246→| Forms/clicks | No | Yes |
   247→| Screenshots | No | Yes |
   248→| Sessions | No | Yes |
   249→| Use case | Static content | Interactive sites |
   250→
   251→**When to use `web_fetch`:**
   252→- Reading documentation
   253→- Fetching API responses
   254→- Scraping static HTML
   255→
   256→**When to use `browser`:**
   257→- Logging into websites
   258→- Filling forms
   259→- Interacting with SPAs
   260→- Sites that require JavaScript
   261→- Taking screenshots
   262→
   263→## Metrics
   264→
   265→When the `metrics` feature is enabled, the browser module records:
   266→
   267→| Metric | Description |
   268→|--------|-------------|
   269→| `moltis_browser_instances_active` | Currently running browsers |
   270→| `moltis_browser_instances_created_total` | Total browsers launched |
   271→| `moltis_browser_instances_destroyed_total` | Total browsers closed |
   272→| `moltis_browser_screenshots_total` | Screenshots taken |
   273→| `moltis_browser_navigation_duration_seconds` | Page load time histogram |
   274→| `moltis_browser_errors_total` | Errors by type |
   275→
   276→## Sandbox Mode
   277→
   278→Browser sandbox mode **automatically follows the session's sandbox mode**. When
   279→a chat session uses sandbox mode (controlled by `[tools.exec.sandbox]`), the
   280→browser tool will also run in a sandboxed container. When the session is not
   281→sandboxed, the browser runs directly on the host.
   282→
   283→### Host Mode
   284→
   285→When the session is not sandboxed (or sandbox mode is "off"), Chrome runs
   286→directly on the host machine. This is faster but the browser has full access
   287→to the host network and filesystem.
   288→
   289→### Sandbox Mode
   290→
   291→When the session is sandboxed, Chrome runs inside a Docker container with:
   292→
   293→- **Network isolation**: Browser can access the internet but not local services
   294→- **Filesystem isolation**: No access to host filesystem
   295→- **Automatic lifecycle**: Container started/stopped with browser session
   296→- **Readiness detection**: Waits for Chrome to be fully ready before connecting
   297→
   298→```toml
   299→[tools.browser]
   300→sandbox_image = "browserless/chrome"  # Container image for sandboxed sessions
   301→```
   302→
   303→Requirements:
   304→- Docker or Apple Container must be installed and running
   305→- The container image is pulled automatically on first use
   306→- Session sandbox mode must be enabled (`[tools.exec.sandbox] mode = "all"`)
   307→
   308→### Moltis Inside Docker (Sibling Containers)
   309→
   310→When Moltis itself runs inside a Docker container, the browser container is
   311→launched as a sibling via the host's Docker socket. By default Moltis connects
   312→to the browser at `127.0.0.1`, which points to the Moltis container's own
   313→loopback — not the host where the browser port is mapped.
   314→
   315→Set `container_host` so Moltis can reach the browser container through the
   316→host's port mapping:
   317→
   318→```toml
   319→[tools.browser]
   320→container_host = "host.docker.internal"   # macOS / Windows Docker Desktop
   321→# container_host = "172.17.0.1"           # Linux Docker bridge gateway IP
   322→```
   323→
   324→On Linux, `host.docker.internal` is not available by default. Use the Docker
   325→bridge gateway IP (typically `172.17.0.1`) or add `--add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway`
   326→to the Moltis container's `docker run` command.
   327→
   328→### Exec Tool Scripts
   329→
   330→If agents need to run browser automation **scripts** (Puppeteer, Playwright,
   331→Selenium) inside the command sandbox, Chromium is included in the default
   332→sandbox packages:
   333→
   334→```bash
   335→# Inside sandbox (via exec tool)
   336→chromium --headless --no-sandbox --dump-dom https://example.com
   337→```
   338→
   339→Or use Puppeteer/Playwright in a Node.js script executed via the `exec` tool.
   340→
   341→## Security Considerations
   342→
   343→### Prompt Injection Risk
   344→
   345→**Important**: Web pages can contain content designed to manipulate LLM behavior
   346→(prompt injection). When the browser tool returns page content to the LLM,
   347→malicious sites could attempt to inject instructions.
   348→
   349→**Mitigations**:
   350→
   351→1. **Domain restrictions**: Use `allowed_domains` to limit navigation to trusted
   352→   sites only. This is the most effective mitigation.
   353→
   354→2. **Review returned content**: The snapshot action returns element text which
   355→   could contain injected prompts. Be cautious with untrusted sites.
   356→
   357→3. **Sandbox mode**: Use sandboxed sessions to run the browser in an isolated
   358→   Docker container for additional security. Browser sandbox follows session
   359→   sandbox mode automatically.
   360→
   361→### Other Security Considerations
   362→
   363→1. **Host vs Sandbox mode**: Browser sandbox mode follows the session's sandbox
   364→   mode. For sandboxed sessions, the browser runs in a Docker container with
   365→   network/filesystem isolation. For non-sandboxed sessions, the browser runs
   366→   on the host with `--no-sandbox` for container compatibility.
   367→
   368→2. **Resource limits**: Browser instances are limited by memory usage (default:
   369→   block when > 90% used). Set `max_instances > 0` for a hard limit.
   370→
   371→3. **Idle cleanup**: Browsers are automatically closed after `idle_timeout_secs`
   372→   of inactivity.
   373→
   374→4. **Network access**: In host mode, the browser has full network access. In
   375→   sandbox mode, the browser can reach the internet but not local services.
   376→   Use firewall rules for additional restrictions.
   377→
   378→5. **Sandbox scripts**: Browser scripts running in the exec sandbox (Puppeteer,
   379→   Playwright) inherit sandbox network restrictions (`no_network: true` by default).
   380→
   381→## Browser Detection
   382→
   383→Moltis automatically detects installed Chromium-based browsers in the following order:
   384→
   385→1. **Custom path** from `chrome_path` config
   386→2. **CHROME environment variable**
   387→3. **Platform-specific app bundles** (macOS/Windows)
   388→   - macOS: `/Applications/Google Chrome.app`, `/Applications/Chromium.app`, etc.
   389→   - Windows: `C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe`, etc.
   390→4. **PATH executables** (fallback): `chrome`, `chromium`, `msedge`, `brave`, etc.
   391→
   392→If no browser is found, Moltis displays platform-specific installation instructions.
   393→
   394→### Supported Browsers
   395→
   396→Any Chromium-based browser works:
   397→- Google Chrome
   398→- Chromium
   399→- Microsoft Edge
   400→- Brave
   401→- Opera
   402→- Vivaldi
   403→- Arc (macOS)
   404→
   405→## Screenshot Display
   406→
   407→When the browser tool takes a screenshot, it's displayed in the chat UI:
   408→
   409→- **Thumbnail view**: Screenshots appear as clickable thumbnails (200×150px max)
   410→- **Fullscreen lightbox**: Click to view full-size with dark overlay
   411→- **Scrollable view**: Long screenshots can be scrolled within the lightbox
   412→- **Download button**: Save screenshot to disk (top of lightbox)
   413→- **Close button**: Click ✕ button, click outside, or press Escape to close
   414→- **HiDPI scaling**: Screenshots display at correct size on Retina displays
   415→
   416→Screenshots are base64-encoded PNGs returned in the tool result. The
   417→`device_scale_factor` config (default: 2.0) controls the rendering resolution
   418→for high-DPI displays.
   419→
   420→### Telegram Integration
   421→
   422→When using the Telegram channel, screenshots are automatically sent to the chat:
   423→
   424→- Images sent as photos when dimensions are within Telegram limits
   425→- Automatically retried as documents for oversized images (aspect ratio > 20)
   426→- Error messages sent to channel if delivery fails
   427→
   428→## Handling Model Errors
   429→
   430→Some models (particularly Claude via GitHub Copilot) occasionally send malformed
   431→tool calls with missing required fields. Moltis handles this gracefully:
   432→
   433→- **Default action**: If `url` is provided but `action` is missing, defaults to
   434→  `navigate`
   435→- **Automatic retry**: The agent loop retries with corrected arguments
   436→- **Muted error display**: Validation errors show as muted/informational cards
   437→  in the UI (60% opacity, gray text) to indicate they're expected, not alarming
   438→
   439→## Troubleshooting
   440→
   441→### Browser not launching
   442→
   443→- Ensure Chrome/Chromium is installed
   444→- Check `chrome_path` in config if using custom location
   445→- Set `CHROME` environment variable to specify browser path
   446→- On Linux, install dependencies: `apt-get install chromium-browser`
   447→- On macOS, if using Homebrew Chromium, prefer installing Google Chrome or Brave
   448→  (the Homebrew chromium wrapper can be unreliable)
   449→
   450→### Elements not found
   451→
   452→- Use `snapshot` to see available elements
   453→- Elements must be visible in the viewport
   454→- Some elements may need scrolling first
   455→
   456→### Timeouts
   457→
   458→- Increase `navigation_timeout_ms` for slow pages
   459→- Use `wait` action to wait for dynamic content
   460→- Check network connectivity
   461→
   462→### High memory usage
   463→
   464→- Browser instances are now limited by memory (blocks at 90% by default)
   465→- Set `max_instances > 0` for a hard limit if preferred
   466→- Lower `idle_timeout_secs` to clean up faster
   467→- Consider enabling headless mode if not already
   468→
   469→### Pool exhaustion
   470→
   471→- Browser tool now auto-tracks session IDs, preventing pool exhaustion from
   472→  LLMs that forget to pass session_id
   473→- If you still hit limits, check `memory_limit_percent` threshold
   474→- Use `close` action when done to free up sessions
   475→

300→            .pool
   301→            .get_or_create(session_id, sandbox, browser)
   302→            .await?;
   303→        let page = self.pool.get_page(&sid).await?;
   304→
   305→        #[cfg(feature = "metrics")]
   306→        let nav_start = Instant::now();
   307→
   308→        // Try navigation, retry with fresh session if connection is dead
   309→        if let Err(e) = page.goto(url).await {
   310→            let nav_err = Error::NavigationFailed(e.to_string());
   311→            if nav_err.is_connection_error() {
   312→                warn!(
   313→                    session_id = sid,
   314→                    "browser connection dead, closing session and retrying"
   315→                );
   316→                let _ = self.pool.close_session(&sid).await;
   317→                // Retry with a fresh session (use same sandbox mode)
   318→                let new_sid = self.pool.get_or_create(None, sandbox, browser).await?;
   319→                let new_page = self.pool.get_page(&new_sid).await?;
   320→                new_page
   321→                    .goto(url)
   322→                    .await
   323→                    .map_err(|e| Error::NavigationFailed(e.to_string()))?;
   324→                // Continue with the new session
   325→                let _ = new_page.wait_for_navigation().await;
   326→                let current_url = new_page.url().await.ok().flatten().unwrap_or_default();
   327→                info!(
   328→                    session_id = new_sid,
   329→                    url = current_url,
   330→                    "navigated to URL (after retry)"
   331→                );
   332→                return Ok((
   333→                    new_sid.clone(),
   334→                    BrowserResponse::success(new_sid, 0, sandbox).with_url(current_url),
   335→                ));
   336→            }
   337→            return Err(nav_err);
   338→        }
   339→
   340→        // Wait for network idle
   341→        let _ = page.wait_for_navigation().await;
   342→
   343→        #[cfg(feature = "metrics")]
   344→        {
   345→            moltis_metrics::histogram!(moltis_metrics::browser::NAVIGATION_DURATION_SECONDS)
   346→                .record(nav_start.elapsed().as_secs_f64());
   347→        }
   348→
   349→        let current_url = page.url().await.ok().flatten().unwrap_or_default();
   350→
   351→        info!(session_id = sid, url = current_url, "navigated to URL");
   352→
   353→        Ok((
   354→            sid.clone(),
   355→            BrowserResponse::success(sid, 0, sandbox).with_url(current_url),
   356→        ))
   357→    }
   358→
   359→    /// Take a screenshot of the page.
   360→    async fn screenshot(
   361→        &self,
   362→        session_id: Option<&str>,
   363→        full_page: bool,
   364→        highlight_ref: Option<u32>,
   365→        sandbox: bool,
   366→        browser: Option<BrowserPreference>,
   367→    ) -> Result<(String, BrowserResponse), Error> {
   368→        let sid = self
   369→            .pool
   370→            .get_or_create(session_id, sandbox, browser)
   371→            .await?;
   372→        let page = self.pool.get_page(&sid).await?;
   373→
   374→        // Optionally highlight an element before screenshot
   375→        if let Some(ref_) = highlight_ref {
   376→            let _ = self.highlight_element(&page, ref_).await;
   377→        }
   378→
   379→        let screenshot = page
   380→            .screenshot(
   381→                chromiumoxide::page::ScreenshotParams::builder()
   382→                    .format(CaptureScreenshotFormat::Png)
   383→                    .full_page(full_page)
   384→                    .build(),
   385→            )
   386→            .await
   387→            .map_err(|e| Error::ScreenshotFailed(e.to_string()))?;
   388→
   389→        // Remove highlight after screenshot
   390→        if highlight_ref.is_some() {
   391→            let _ = self.remove_highlights(&page).await;
   392→        }
   393→
   394→        // Use data URI format so the sanitizer can strip it for LLM context
   395→        // while the UI can still display it as an image
   396→        let data_uri = format!("data:image/png;base64,{}", BASE64.encode(&screenshot));
   397→
   398→        #[cfg(feature = "metrics")]
   399→        moltis_metrics::counter!(moltis_metrics::browser::SCREENSHOTS_TOTAL).increment(1);
   400→
   401→        // Calculate approximate dimensions from PNG data (width/height are in bytes 16-23)
   402→        let (width, height) = if screenshot.len() > 24 {
   403→            let w = u32::from_be_bytes([
   404→                screenshot[16],
   405→                screenshot[17],
   406→                screenshot[18],
   407→                screenshot[19],
   408→            ]);
   409→            let h = u32::from_be_bytes([
   410→                screenshot[20],
   411→                screenshot[21],
   412→                screenshot[22],
   413→                screenshot[23],
   414→            ]);
   415→            (w, h)
   416→        } else {
   417→            (0, 0)
   418→        };
   419→
   420→        info!(
   421→            session_id = sid,
   422→            bytes = screenshot.len(),
   423→            width,
   424→            height,
   425→            full_page,
   426→            "took screenshot"
   427→        );
   428→
   429→        Ok((
   430→            sid.clone(),
   431→            BrowserResponse::success(sid, 0, sandbox)
   432→                .with_screenshot(data_uri, self.config.device_scale_factor),
   433→        ))
   434→    }
   435→
   436→    /// Get a DOM snapshot with element references.
   437→    ///
   438→    /// Stale-connection errors are detected centrally in `execute_action()`.
   439→    async fn snapshot(
   440→        &self,
   441→        session_id: Option<&str>,
   442→        sandbox: bool,
   443→        browser: Option<BrowserPreference>,
   444→    ) -> Result<(String, BrowserResponse), Error> {
   445→        let sid = self
   446→            .pool
   447→            .get_or_create(session_id, sandbox, browser)
   448→            .await?;
   449→        let page = self.pool.get_page(&sid).await?;
   450→
   451→        let snapshot = extract_snapshot(&page).await?;
   452→
   453→        debug!(
   454→            session_id = sid,
   455→            elements = snapshot.elements.len(),
   456→            "extracted snapshot"
   457→        );
   458→
   459→        Ok((

   465→    /// Click an element by reference.
   466→    async fn click(
   467→        &self,
   468→        session_id: Option<&str>,
   469→        ref_: u32,
   470→        sandbox: bool,
   471→    ) -> Result<(String, BrowserResponse), Error> {
   472→        let sid = require_session(session_id, "click")?;
   473→
   474→        let page = self.pool.get_page(&sid).await?;
   475→
   476→        // Scroll element into view first
   477→        scroll_element_into_view(&page, ref_).await?;
   478→
   479→        // Small delay for scroll to complete
   480→        tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;
   481→
   482→        // Find element center
   483→        let (x, y) = find_element_by_ref(&page, ref_).await?;
   484→
   485→        // Dispatch mouse events
   486→        let press_cmd = DispatchMouseEventParams::builder()
   487→            .r#type(DispatchMouseEventType::MousePressed)
   488→            .x(x)
   489→            .y(y)
   490→            .button(MouseButton::Left)
   491→            .click_count(1)
   492→            .build()
   493→            .map_err(|e| Error::Cdp(e.to_string()))?;
   494→        page.execute(press_cmd)
   495→            .await
   496→            .map_err(|e| Error::Cdp(e.to_string()))?;
   497→
   498→        let release_cmd = DispatchMouseEventParams::builder()
   499→            .r#type(DispatchMouseEventType::MouseReleased)
   500→            .x(x)
   501→            .y(y)
   502→            .button(MouseButton::Left)
   503→            .click_count(1)
   504→            .build()
   505→            .map_err(|e| Error::Cdp(e.to_string()))?;
   506→        page.execute(release_cmd)
   507→            .await
   508→            .map_err(|e| Error::Cdp(e.to_string()))?;
   509→
   510→        debug!(
   511→            session_id = sid,
   512→            ref_ = ref_,
   513→            x = x,
   514→            y = y,
   515→            "clicked element"
   516→        );
   517→
   518→        Ok((sid.clone(), BrowserResponse::success(sid, 0, sandbox)))
   519→    }

   521→    /// Type text into an element.
   522→    async fn type_text(
   523→        &self,
   524→        session_id: Option<&str>,
   525→        ref_: u32,
   526→        text: &str,
   527→        sandbox: bool,
   528→    ) -> Result<(String, BrowserResponse), Error> {
   529→        let sid = require_session(session_id, "type")?;
   530→
   531→        let page = self.pool.get_page(&sid).await?;
   532→
   533→        // Focus the element
   534→        focus_element_by_ref(&page, ref_).await?;
   535→
   536→        // Type each character
   537→        for c in text.chars() {
   538→            let key_down = DispatchKeyEventParams::builder()
   539→                .r#type(DispatchKeyEventType::KeyDown)
   540→                .text(c.to_string())
   541→                .build()
   542→                .map_err(|e| Error::Cdp(e.to_string()))?;
   543→            page.execute(key_down)
   544→                .await
   545→                .map_err(|e| Error::Cdp(e.to_string()))?;
   546→
   547→            let key_up = DispatchKeyEventParams::builder()
   548→                .r#type(DispatchKeyEventType::KeyUp)
   549→                .text(c.to_string())
   550→                .build()
   551→                .map_err(|e| Error::Cdp(e.to_string()))?;
   552→            page.execute(key_up)
   553→                .await
   554→                .map_err(|e| Error::Cdp(e.to_string()))?;
   555→        }
   556→
   557→        debug!(
   558→            session_id = sid,
   559→            ref_ = ref_,
   560→            chars = text.len(),
   561→            "typed text"
   562→        );
   563→
   564→        Ok((sid.clone(), BrowserResponse::success(sid, 0, sandbox)))
   565→    }

   567→    /// Scroll the page or an element.
   568→    async fn scroll(
   569→        &self,
   570→        session_id: Option<&str>,
   571→        ref_: Option<u32>,
   572→        x: i32,
   573→        y: i32,
   574→        sandbox: bool,
   575→    ) -> Result<(String, BrowserResponse), Error> {
   576→        let sid = require_session(session_id, "scroll")?;
   577→
   578→        let page = self.pool.get_page(&sid).await?;
   579→
   580→        let js = if let Some(ref_) = ref_ {
   581→            format!(
   582→                r#"(() => {{
   583→                    const el = document.querySelector(`[data-moltis-ref="{ref_}"]`);
   584→                    if (el) el.scrollBy({x}, {y});
   585→                    return !!el;
   586→                }})()"#
   587→            )
   588→        } else {
   589→            format!("window.scrollBy({x}, {y}); true")
   590→        };
   591→
   592→        page.evaluate(js.as_str())
   593→            .await
   594→            .map_err(|e| Error::JsEvalFailed(e.to_string()))?;
   595→
   596→        debug!(session_id = sid, ref_ = ?ref_, x = x, y = y, "scrolled");
   597→
   598→        Ok((sid.clone(), BrowserResponse::success(sid, 0, sandbox)))
   599→    }

   601→    /// Execute JavaScript in the page context.
   602→    async fn evaluate(
   603→        &self,
   604→        session_id: Option<&str>,
   605→        code: &str,
   606→        sandbox: bool,
   607→    ) -> Result<(String, BrowserResponse), Error> {
   608→        let sid = require_session(session_id, "evaluate")?;
   609→
   610→        let page = self.pool.get_page(&sid).await?;
   611→
   612→        let result: serde_json::Value = page
   613→            .evaluate(code)
   614→            .await
   615→            .map_err(|e| Error::JsEvalFailed(e.to_string()))?
   616→            .into_value()
   617→            .map_err(|e| Error::JsEvalFailed(format!("{e:?}")))?;
   618→
   619→        debug!(session_id = sid, "evaluated JavaScript");
   620→
   621→        Ok((
   622→            sid.clone(),
   623→            BrowserResponse::success(sid, 0, sandbox).with_result(result),
   624→        ))
   625→    }

   627→    /// Wait for an element to appear.
   628→    async fn wait(
   629→        &self,
   630→        session_id: Option<&str>,
   631→        selector: Option<String>,
   632→        ref_: Option<u32>,
   633→        timeout_ms: u64,
   634→        sandbox: bool,
   635→    ) -> Result<(String, BrowserResponse), Error> {
   636→        let sid = require_session(session_id, "wait")?;
   637→
   638→        let page = self.pool.get_page(&sid).await?;
   639→
   640→        let check_js = if let Some(ref selector) = selector {
   641→            format!(
   642→                r#"document.querySelector({}) !== null"#,
   643→                serde_json::to_string(selector).map_err(|e| Error::Cdp(e.to_string()))?
   644→            )
   645→        } else if let Some(ref_) = ref_ {
   646→            format!(r#"document.querySelector('[data-moltis-ref="{ref_}"]') !== null"#)
   647→        } else {
   648→            return Err(Error::InvalidAction("wait requires selector or ref".into()));
   649→        };
   650→
   651→        let deadline = Instant::now() + Duration::from_millis(timeout_ms);
   652→        let interval = Duration::from_millis(100);
   653→
   654→        while Instant::now() < deadline {
   655→            let found: bool = page
   656→                .evaluate(check_js.as_str())
   657→                .await
   658→                .map_err(|e| Error::JsEvalFailed(e.to_string()))?
   659→                .into_value()
   660→                .unwrap_or(false);
   661→
   662→            if found {
   663→                debug!(session_id = sid, "element found");
   664→                return Ok((sid.clone(), BrowserResponse::success(sid, 0, sandbox)));
   665→            }
   666→
   667→            tokio::time::sleep(interval).await;
   668→        }
   669→
   670→        Err(Error::Timeout(format!(
   671→            "element not found after {}ms",
   672→            timeout_ms
   673→        )))
   674→    }

1679→pub struct BrowserConfig {
  1680→    /// Whether browser support is enabled.
  1681→    pub enabled: bool,
  1682→    /// Path to Chrome/Chromium binary (auto-detected if not set).
  1683→    pub chrome_path: Option<String>,
  1684→    /// Whether to run in headless mode.
  1685→    pub headless: bool,
  1686→    /// Default viewport width.
  1687→    pub viewport_width: u32,
  1688→    /// Default viewport height.
  1689→    pub viewport_height: u32,
  1690→    /// Device scale factor for HiDPI/Retina displays.
  1691→    /// 1.0 = standard, 2.0 = Retina/HiDPI, 3.0 = 3x scaling.
  1692→    pub device_scale_factor: f64,
  1693→    /// Maximum concurrent browser instances (0 = unlimited, limited by memory).
  1694→    pub max_instances: usize,
  1695→    /// System memory usage threshold (0-100) above which new instances are blocked.
  1696→    /// Default is 90 (block new instances when memory > 90% used).
  1697→    pub memory_limit_percent: u8,
  1698→    /// Instance idle timeout in seconds before closing.
  1699→    pub idle_timeout_secs: u64,
  1700→    /// Default navigation timeout in milliseconds.
  1701→    pub navigation_timeout_ms: u64,
  1702→    /// User agent string (uses default if not set).
  1703→    pub user_agent: Option<String>,
  1704→    /// Additional Chrome arguments.
  1705→    #[serde(default)]
  1706→    pub chrome_args: Vec<String>,
  1707→    /// Docker image to use for sandboxed browser.
  1708→    /// Default: "browserless/chrome" - a purpose-built headless Chrome container.
  1709→    /// Sandbox mode is controlled per-session via the request, not globally.
  1710→    #[serde(default = "default_sandbox_image")]
  1711→    pub sandbox_image: String,
  1712→    /// Allowed domains for navigation. Empty list means all domains allowed.
  1713→    /// When set, the browser will refuse to navigate to non-matching domains.
  1714→    /// Supports wildcards: "*.example.com" matches subdomains.
  1715→    #[serde(default)]
  1716→    pub allowed_domains: Vec<String>,
  1717→    /// Total system RAM threshold (MB) below which memory-saving Chrome flags
  1718→    /// are injected automatically. Set to 0 to disable. Default: 2048.
  1719→    #[serde(default = "default_low_memory_threshold_mb")]
  1720→    pub low_memory_threshold_mb: u64,
  1721→    /// Whether to persist the Chrome user profile across sessions.
  1722→    /// When enabled, cookies, auth state, and local storage survive browser restarts.
  1723→    /// Profile is stored at `data_dir()/browser/profile/` unless `profile_dir` overrides it.
  1724→    #[serde(default = "default_persist_profile")]
  1725→    pub persist_profile: bool,
  1726→    /// Custom path for the persistent Chrome profile directory.
  1727→    /// When set, `persist_profile` is implicitly true.
  1728→    /// If not set and `persist_profile` is true, defaults to `data_dir()/browser/profile/`.
  1729→    pub profile_dir: Option<String>,
  1730→    /// Hostname or IP used to connect to the browser container from the host.
  1731→    /// Default: "127.0.0.1" (localhost). When running Moltis itself inside Docker,
  1732→    /// set this to "host.docker.internal" or the Docker bridge gateway IP so
  1733→    /// Moltis can reach the sibling browser container via the host's port mapping.
  1734→    #[serde(default = "default_container_host")]
  1735→    pub container_host: String,
  1736→}

  1738→fn default_sandbox_image() -> String {

  1740→}
  1741→
  1742→const fn default_low_memory_threshold_mb() -> u64 {

  1745→
  1746→const fn default_persist_profile() -> bool {
  1747→    true
  1748→}
  1749→
  1750→fn default_container_host() -> String {

    70→#[cfg(feature = "mdns")]
    71→fn instance_slug(config: &moltis_config::MoltisConfig) -> String {
    72→    let mut raw_name = config.identity.name.clone();
    73→    if let Some(file_identity) = moltis_config::load_identity()
    74→        && file_identity.name.is_some()
    75→    {
    76→        raw_name = file_identity.name;
    77→    }
    78→
    79→    let base = raw_name
    80→        .unwrap_or_else(|| "moltis".to_string())
    81→        .to_lowercase();
    82→    let mut out = String::new();
    83→    let mut last_dash = false;
    84→    for ch in base.chars() {
    85→        let mapped = if ch.is_ascii_alphanumeric() {
    86→            ch
    87→        } else {
    88→            '-'
    89→        };
    90→        if mapped == '-' {
    91→            if !last_dash {
    92→                out.push(mapped);
    93→            }
    94→            last_dash = true;
    95→        } else {
    96→            out.push(mapped);
    97→            last_dash = false;
    98→        }
    99→    }
   100→    let out = out.trim_matches('-').to_string();
   101→    if out.is_empty() {
   102→        "moltis".to_string()
   103→    } else {
   104→        out
   105→    }
   106→}

   110→#[derive(Clone)]
   111→pub struct AppState {
   112→    pub gateway: Arc<GatewayState>,
   113→    pub methods: Arc<MethodRegistry>,
   114→    pub request_throttle: Arc<crate::request_throttle::RequestThrottle>,
   115→    pub webauthn_registry: Option<SharedWebAuthnRegistry>,
   116→    #[cfg(feature = "push-notifications")]
   117→    pub push_service: Option<Arc<moltis_gateway::push::PushService>>,
