   122→/// Function signature for adding extra routes (e.g. web-UI) to the gateway.
   123→pub type RouteEnhancer = fn() -> Router<AppState>;
   124→
   125→// ── Server startup ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   126→
   127→/// Build the API routes (shared between both build_gateway_app versions).
   128→///
   129→/// Auth is enforced by `auth_gate` middleware on the whole router — these
   130→/// routes no longer carry their own auth layer.
   131→/// Add feature-specific routes to API routes.
   132→/// Build the CORS layer with dynamic host-based origin validation.
   133→///
   134→/// Instead of `allow_origin(Any)`, this validates the `Origin` header against the
   135→/// request's `Host` header using the same `is_same_origin` logic as the WebSocket
   136→/// CSWSH protection. This is secure for Docker/cloud deployments where the hostname
   137→/// is unknown at build time — the server dynamically allows its own origin at
   138→/// request time.
   139→fn build_cors_layer() -> CorsLayer {
   140→    CorsLayer::new()
   141→        .allow_origin(AllowOrigin::predicate(
   142→            |origin: &axum::http::HeaderValue, parts: &axum::http::request::Parts| {
   143→                let origin_str = origin.to_str().unwrap_or("");
   144→                let host = parts
   145→                    .headers
   146→                    .get(axum::http::header::HOST)
   147→                    .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
   148→                    .unwrap_or("");
   149→                is_same_origin(origin_str, host)
   150→            },
   151→        ))
   152→        .allow_methods(Any)
   153→        .allow_headers(Any)
   154→}
   155→
   156→/// 2 MiB global request body limit — sufficient for any JSON API payload, small
   157→/// enough to limit abuse. The upload endpoint has its own 25 MiB limit.
   158→const REQUEST_BODY_LIMIT: usize = 2 * 1024 * 1024;
   159→
   160→/// Apply the full middleware stack to the router.
   161→///
   162→/// Layer order (outermost → innermost for requests):
   163→/// 1. `CatchPanicLayer` — converts handler panics to 500s
   164→/// 2. `SetSensitiveHeadersLayer` — marks Authorization/Cookie as redacted
   165→/// 3. `SetRequestIdLayer` — generates x-request-id before tracing
   166→/// 4. `TraceLayer` (optional) — logs requests with redacted headers + request ID
   167→/// 5. `CorsLayer` — handles preflight; logged by trace
   168→/// 6. `PropagateRequestIdLayer` — copies x-request-id to response
   169→/// 7. Security response headers — X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, etc.
   170→/// 8. `RequestBodyLimitLayer` — rejects oversized bodies
   171→/// 9. `CompressionLayer` (innermost) — compresses response body
   172→fn apply_middleware_stack<S>(
   173→    router: Router<S>,
   174→    cors: CorsLayer,
   175→    http_request_logs: bool,
   176→) -> Router<S>
   177→where
   178→    S: Clone + Send + Sync + 'static,
   179→{
   180→    use axum::http::{HeaderValue, header};
   181→
   182→    // Inner layers: compression, body limit, security headers, request ID propagation.
   183→    let router = router
   184→        .layer(CompressionLayer::new())
   185→        .layer(tower_http::limit::RequestBodyLimitLayer::new(
   186→            REQUEST_BODY_LIMIT,
   187→        ))
   188→        .layer(SetResponseHeaderLayer::overriding(
   189→            header::HeaderName::from_static("x-content-type-options"),
   190→            HeaderValue::from_static("nosniff"),
   191→        ))
   192→        .layer(SetResponseHeaderLayer::overriding(
   193→            header::HeaderName::from_static("x-frame-options"),
   194→            HeaderValue::from_static("sameorigin"),
   195→        ))
   196→        .layer(SetResponseHeaderLayer::overriding(
   197→            header::HeaderName::from_static("referrer-policy"),
   198→            HeaderValue::from_static("strict-origin-when-cross-origin"),
   199→        ))
   200→        .layer(PropagateRequestIdLayer::x_request_id())

1→//! Browser action types and request/response structures.
     2→
     3→use std::fmt;
     4→
     5→use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};
     6→
     7→/// Browser action to perform.
     8→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]
     9→#[serde(tag = "action", rename_all = "snake_case")]
    10→pub enum BrowserAction {
    11→    /// Navigate to a URL.
    12→    Navigate { url: String },
    13→
    14→    /// Take a screenshot of the current page.
    15→    Screenshot {
    16→        #[serde(default)]
    17→        full_page: bool,
    18→        /// Optional: highlight element by ref before screenshot.
    19→        #[serde(default)]
    20→        highlight_ref: Option<u32>,
    21→    },
    22→
    23→    /// Get a DOM snapshot with numbered element references.
    24→    Snapshot,
    25→
    26→    /// Click an element by its reference number.
    27→    Click { ref_: u32 },
    28→
    29→    /// Type text into an element.
    30→    Type { ref_: u32, text: String },
    31→
    32→    /// Scroll the page or an element.
    33→    Scroll {
    34→        /// Element ref to scroll (None = viewport).
    35→        #[serde(default)]
    36→        ref_: Option<u32>,
    37→        /// Horizontal scroll delta.
    38→        #[serde(default)]
    39→        x: i32,
    40→        /// Vertical scroll delta.
    41→        #[serde(default)]
    42→        y: i32,
    43→    },
    44→
    45→    /// Execute JavaScript in the page context.
    46→    Evaluate { code: String },
    47→
    48→    /// Wait for an element to appear (by CSS selector or ref).
    49→    Wait {
    50→        #[serde(default)]
    51→        selector: Option<String>,
    52→        #[serde(default)]
    53→        ref_: Option<u32>,
    54→        #[serde(default = "default_wait_timeout_ms")]
    55→        timeout_ms: u64,
    56→    },
    57→
    58→    /// Get the current page URL.
    59→    GetUrl,
    60→
    61→    /// Get the page title.
    62→    GetTitle,
    63→
    64→    /// Go back in history.
    65→    Back,
    66→
    67→    /// Go forward in history.
    68→    Forward,
    69→
    70→    /// Refresh the page.
    71→    Refresh,
    72→
    73→    /// Close the browser session.
    74→    Close,
    75→
    76→    /// Start streaming screenshots of the page (CDP screencast).
    77→    StartScreencast {
    78→        /// JPEG quality 1-100 (default 60).
    79→        #[serde(default = "default_screencast_quality")]
    80→        quality: u8,
    81→        /// Max width to downscale to (default 1280).
    82→        #[serde(default = "default_screencast_max_width")]
    83→        max_width: u32,
    84→        /// Max height to downscale to (default 800).
    85→        #[serde(default = "default_screencast_max_height")]
    86→        max_height: u32,
    87→    },
    88→
    89→    /// Stop the screencast stream.
    90→    StopScreencast,
    91→
    92→    /// Send a mouse event directly to the browser page.
    93→    MouseInput {
    94→        /// X coordinate in CSS pixels.
    95→        x: f64,
    96→        /// Y coordinate in CSS pixels.
    97→        y: f64,
    98→        /// Mouse event type.
    99→        event_type: MouseInputType,
   100→        /// Mouse button (default: left).
   101→        #[serde(default)]
   102→        button: MouseInputButton,
   103→        /// Click count (default: 1).
   104→        #[serde(default = "default_click_count")]
   105→        click_count: u32,
   106→    },
   107→
   108→    /// Send a keyboard event directly to the browser page.
   109→    KeyboardInput {
   110→        /// Keyboard event type.
   111→        event_type: KeyInputType,
   112→        /// Key identifier (e.g. "Enter", "a", "ArrowDown").
   113→        #[serde(default)]
   114→        key: Option<String>,
   115→        /// Text to insert (for char/keyDown events).
   116→        #[serde(default)]
   117→        text: Option<String>,
   118→        /// Physical key code (e.g. "KeyA", "Enter").
   119→        #[serde(default)]
   120→        code: Option<String>,
   121→        /// Modifier bitmask: Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta=4, Shift=8.
   122→        #[serde(default)]
   123→        modifiers: Option<i64>,
   124→    },
   125→
   126→    /// Export cookies from the browser session.
   127→    ExportCookies {
   128→        /// Optional domain filter (e.g. "example.com").
   129→        #[serde(default)]
   130→        domain: Option<String>,
   131→    },
   132→
   133→    /// Import cookies into the browser session.
   134→    ImportCookies {
   135→        /// Cookies to set.
   136→        cookies: Vec<CookieParam>,
   137→    },
   138→}
   139→
   140→fn default_wait_timeout_ms() -> u64 {
   141→    30000
   142→}
   143→
   144→fn default_screencast_quality() -> u8 {
   145→    60
   146→}
   147→
   148→fn default_screencast_max_width() -> u32 {
   149→    1280
   150→}

150→}
   151→
   152→fn default_screencast_max_height() -> u32 {
   153→    800
   154→}
   155→
   156→fn default_click_count() -> u32 {
   157→    1
   158→}

   224→/// Known Chromium-family browser engines we can launch.
   225→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   226→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   227→pub enum BrowserKind {
   228→    Chrome,
   229→    Chromium,
   230→    Edge,
   231→    Brave,
   232→    Opera,
   233→    Vivaldi,
   234→    Arc,
   235→    Custom,
   236→}
   237→
   238→impl BrowserKind {
   239→    pub fn as_str(self) -> &'static str {
   240→        match self {
   241→            Self::Chrome => "chrome",
   242→            Self::Chromium => "chromium",
   243→            Self::Edge => "edge",
   244→            Self::Brave => "brave",
   245→            Self::Opera => "opera",
   246→            Self::Vivaldi => "vivaldi",
   247→            Self::Arc => "arc",
   248→            Self::Custom => "custom",
   249→        }
   250→    }
   251→}
   252→
   253→impl fmt::Display for BrowserKind {
   254→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
   255→        f.write_str(self.as_str())
   256→    }
   257→}
   258→
   259→/// Preferred browser for a request.
   260→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
   261→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   262→pub enum BrowserPreference {
   263→    #[default]
   264→    Auto,
   265→    Chrome,
   266→    Chromium,
   267→    Edge,
   268→    Brave,
   269→    Opera,
   270→    Vivaldi,
   271→    Arc,
   272→}
   273→
   274→impl BrowserPreference {
   275→    pub fn preferred_kind(self) -> Option<BrowserKind> {
   276→        match self {
   277→            Self::Auto => None,
   278→            Self::Chrome => Some(BrowserKind::Chrome),
   279→            Self::Chromium => Some(BrowserKind::Chromium),
   280→            Self::Edge => Some(BrowserKind::Edge),
   281→            Self::Brave => Some(BrowserKind::Brave),
   282→            Self::Opera => Some(BrowserKind::Opera),
   283→            Self::Vivaldi => Some(BrowserKind::Vivaldi),
   284→            Self::Arc => Some(BrowserKind::Arc),
   285→        }
   286→    }
   287→}
   288→
   289→impl fmt::Display for BrowserPreference {
   290→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
   291→        match self.preferred_kind() {
   292→            Some(kind) => kind.fmt(f),
   293→            None => f.write_str("auto"),
   294→        }
   295→    }
   296→}
   297→
   298→impl fmt::Display for BrowserAction {
   299→    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
   300→        match self {
   301→            Self::Navigate { url } => write!(f, "navigate({})", url),
   302→            Self::Screenshot { full_page, .. } => {
   303→                if *full_page {
   304→                    write!(f, "screenshot(full_page)")
   305→                } else {
   306→                    write!(f, "screenshot")
   307→                }
   308→            },
   309→            Self::Snapshot => write!(f, "snapshot"),
   310→            Self::Click { ref_ } => write!(f, "click(ref={})", ref_),
   311→            Self::Type { ref_, .. } => write!(f, "type(ref={})", ref_),
   312→            Self::Scroll { ref_, x, y } => match ref_ {
   313→                Some(r) => write!(f, "scroll(ref={}, x={}, y={})", r, x, y),
   314→                None => write!(f, "scroll(x={}, y={})", x, y),
   315→            },
   316→            Self::Evaluate { .. } => write!(f, "evaluate"),

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the browser configuration. Let me create a summary document with all the information I've gathered.

## Browser Configuration in Moltis - Complete Overview

Based on my search of the codebase, here's what's currently configurable in browser automation:

### **Configuration Sources**

1. **Schema Definition**: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/config/src/schema.rs` (lines 1679-1777)
2. **Template/Documentation**: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/config/src/template.rs` (lines 413-439)
3. **Type Definitions**: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/browser/src/types.rs` (lines 570-672)
4. **Documentation**: `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/docs/src/browser-automation.md`

---

### **BrowserConfig Struct Fields**

In `crates/config/src/schema.rs` and `crates/browser/src/types.rs`:

#### **Core Functionality**
- **`enabled`** (bool): Enable/disable browser support globally
  - Default: `true`

#### **Binary & Paths**
- **`chrome_path`** (Option<String>): Custom Chrome/Chromium binary path
  - Default: `None` (auto-detected)
- **`profile_dir`** (Option<String>): Custom path for persistent Chrome user profile
  - Default: `None` (uses `~/.moltis/browser/profile/` when `persist_profile=true`)

#### **Display & Viewport**
- **`headless`** (bool): Run without visible window
  - Default: `true`
- **`viewport_width`** (u32): Default page viewport width in pixels
  - Default: `2560` (QHD)
- **`viewport_height`** (u32): Default page viewport height in pixels
  - Default: `1440`
- **`device_scale_factor`** (f64): HiDPI/Retina scaling factor
  - Default: `2.0` (Retina/HiDPI)
  - Values: `1.0` = standard, `2.0` = Retina, `3.0` = 3x scaling

#### **Profile Persistence**
- **`persist_profile`** (bool): Keep Chrome profile (cookies, auth, local storage) across sessions
  - Default: `true`
- When enabled, profile stored at `data_dir()/browser/profile/` unless `profile_dir` overrides it

#### **Pool & Resource Management**
- **`max_instances`** (usize): Maximum concurrent browser instances
  - Default: `0` (unlimited, limited only by memory)
  - Set `> 0` for hard limit
- **`memory_limit_percent`** (u8): System memory threshold (0-100) to block new instances
  - Default: `90` (block when RAM usage > 90%)
- **`low_memory_threshold_mb`** (u64): RAM threshold (MB) below which memory-saving Chrome flags are auto-injected
  - Default: `2048` (2GB)
  - Set to `0` to disable
- **`idle_timeout_secs`** (u64): Close idle browser instances after N seconds
  - Default: `300` (5 minutes)

#### **Timeouts**
- **`navigation_timeout_ms`** (u64): Page load timeout in milliseconds
  - Default: `30000` (30 seconds)

#### **User Agent & Arguments**
- **`user_agent`** (Option<String>): Custom User-Agent string
  - Default: `None` (uses Chromium default)
- **`chrome_args`** (Vec<String>): Additional Chrome command-line arguments
  - Default: `[]` (empty)
  - Example: `["--disable-extensions", "--disable-gpu"]`

#### **Security & Domain Restrictions**
- **`allowed_domains`** (Vec<String>): Domains browser can navigate to
  - Default: `[]` (all domains allowed)
  - Supports wildcards: `"*.github.com"` matches any subdomain of github.com and also `github.com` itself
  - Empty list = unrestricted navigation
  - Examples from template:
    ```toml
    allowed_domains = [
        "docs.example.com",     # Exact match
        "*.github.com",         # Wildcard: matches subdomains + base
        "localhost",            # Allow localhost
        "127.0.0.1",
    ]
    ```

#### **Sandbox & Containerization**
- **`sandbox`** (bool, per-request): Whether to run in Docker/Apple Container
  - Default: `false` (runs on host)
  - **Important**: Sandbox mode for browser follows the session's sandbox mode (controlled by `[tools.exec.sandbox] mode`)
- **`sandbox_image`** (String): Docker image for sandboxed browser
  - Default: `"browserless/chrome"` (purpose-built headless Chrome container)
- **`container_host`** (String): Hostname/IP to reach browser container
  - Default: `"127.0.0.1"` (localhost)
  - Set to `"host.docker.internal"` when Moltis runs inside Docker with sibling browser container
  - On Linux Docker: Use Docker bridge gateway IP (typically `172.17.0.1`) or add `--add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway` to Moltis container

---

### **Browser Actions (Per-Request)**

In `crates/browser/src/types.rs` - `BrowserAction` enum:

#### **Navigation & State**
- `Navigate { url }` - Navigate to a URL
- `GetUrl` - Get current page URL
- `GetTitle` - Get page title
- `Back` - Go back in browser history
- `Forward` - Go forward in browser history
- `Refresh` - Reload the page
- `Close` - Close browser session

#### **Interaction**
- `Click { ref_: u32 }` - Click element by reference
- `Type { ref_: u32, text }` - Type text into element
- `Scroll { ref_, x, y }` - Scroll page/element (x=horizontal, y=vertical delta)
- `MouseInput { x, y, event_type, button, click_count }` - Direct mouse input
  - Event types: `mousePressed`, `mouseReleased`, `mouseMoved`, `mouseWheel`
  - Buttons: `left` (default), `right`, `middle`
- `KeyboardInput { event_type, key, text, code, modifiers }` - Direct keyboard input
  - Event types: `KeyDown`, `KeyUp`, `Char`

#### **Content & Inspection**
- `Snapshot` - Get DOM with interactive element references
- `Screenshot { full_page, highlight_ref? }` - Capture page image
  - `full_page`: true = full scrollable page, false = viewport only
  - `highlight_ref`: Optional element reference to highlight before capture
- `Evaluate { code }` - Execute JavaScript in page context
- `Wait { selector?, ref_?, timeout_ms? }` - Wait for element to appear
  - Default timeout: `30000ms`

#### **Cookies**
- `ExportCookies { domain? }` - Export cookies (optionally filtered by domain)
- `ImportCookies { cookies }` - Import cookies into session

#### **Streaming**
- `StartScreencast { quality?, max_width?, max_height? }` - Stream screenshots via CDP screencast
  - Quality: 1-100 (default 60 JPEG)
  - Max width: default 1280
  - Max height: default 800
- `StopScreencast` - Stop the screencast stream

---

### **BrowserRequest (Per-Request Options)**

In `crates/browser/src/types.rs` - `BrowserRequest` struct:

- **`session_id`** (Option<String>): Browser session ID
  - Optional - creates new if missing
  - Auto-tracked by tool so LLM doesn't need to pass it explicitly
- **`action`** (BrowserAction): The action to perform (flattened in JSON)
- **`timeout_ms`** (u64): Global timeout for this action
  - Default: `60000ms` (60 seconds)
- **`sandbox`** (Option<bool>): Run in Docker container for this request
  - Default: `None` (uses host mode)
- **`browser`** (Option<BrowserPreference>): Preferred browser for host mode
  - Options: `auto` (default), `chrome`, `chromium`, `edge`, `brave`, `opera`, `vivaldi`, `arc`
  - `auto` picks first detected installed browser

---

### **Cookie Handling**

In `crates/browser/src/types.rs`:

#### **CookieParam (for import)**
```rust
{
    "name": String,
    "value": String,
    "domain": Option<String>,
    "path": Option<String>,
    "secure": bool (default false),
    "http_only": bool (default false),
    "expires": Option<f64> (Unix timestamp in seconds),
    "same_site": Option<String>,
}
```

#### **ExportedCookie (returned by export)**
```rust
{
    "name": String,
    "value": String,
    "domain": String,
    "path": String,
    "secure": bool,
    "http_only": bool,
    "expires": f64,
    "same_site": String,
    "size": u32,
}
```

---

### **Browser Kind/Selection**

In `crates/browser/src/types.rs` - `BrowserKind` and `BrowserPreference` enums:

**Supported Chromium-based browsers:**
- Chrome
- Chromium
- Edge
- Brave
- Opera
- Vivaldi
- Arc (macOS)
- Auto (auto-detect, picks first found)

---

### **Configuration Example (moltis.toml)**

From the template (`crates/config/src/template.rs` lines 413-439):

```toml
[tools.browser]
enabled = true                    # Enable browser support
headless = true                   # Run without visible window
viewport_width = 2560             # Default viewport width
viewport_height = 1440            # Default viewport height
device_scale_factor = 2.0         # HiDPI/Retina scaling

# Pool management
max_instances = 3                 # Maximum concurrent instances
idle_timeout_secs = 300           # Close idle browsers after 5 min
navigation_timeout_ms = 30000     # Page load timeout

# Optional customization
# chrome_path = "/path/to/chrome"  # Custom Chrome path
# user_agent = "Custom UA"         # Custom user agent
# chrome_args = []                 # Extra args

# Domain restrictions
allowed_domains = [
    "docs.example.com",           # Exact match
    "*.github.com",               # Wildcard
    "localhost",
    "127.0.0.1",
]

# Sandbox/container settings
# sandbox = false                 # Run browser in Docker
# sandbox_image = "browserless/chrome"
# container_host = "127.0.0.1"   # or "host.docker.internal" in Docker

# Profile persistence
# persist_profile = true          # Keep cookies/auth across sessions
# profile_dir = "/custom/path"    # Custom profile location
```

---

### **Key Missing Features (Not Currently Configurable)**

Based on the config schema and code review:

1. **No per-domain profile isolation** - Single profile for all domains
2. **No cookie restrictions per action** - Can only export/import all or none
3. **No proxy configuration** - No per-request or global proxy settings
4. **No request interception** - Can't block/modify network requests
5. **No geolocation/timezone override** - Uses host system defaults
6. **No viewport profiles** - Only single configurable viewport size
7. **No authentication presets** - No built-in auth profiles or credential management
8. **No screenshot format options** - Only PNG base64 output

---

### **Files to Reference**

- `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/config/src/schema.rs` (lines 1679-1777)
- `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/browser/src/types.rs` (lines 193-828)
- `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/docs/src/browser-automation.md`
- `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/config/src/template.rs` (lines 413-439)

```toml
viewport_height = 1440            # Default viewport height
device_scale_factor = 2.0         # HiDPI/Retina scaling

# Pool management
max_instances = 3                 # Maximum concurrent instances
idle_timeout_secs = 300           # Close idle browsers after 5 min
navigation_timeout_ms = 30000     # Page load timeout

# Optional customization
# chrome_path = "/path/to/chrome"  # Custom Chrome path
# user_agent = "Custom UA"         # Custom user agent
# chrome_args = []                 # Extra args

# Domain restrictions
allowed_domains = [
    "docs.example.com",           # Exact match
    "*.github.com",               # Wildcard
    "localhost",
    "127.0.0.1",
]

# Sandbox/container settings
# sandbox = false                 # Run browser in Docker
# sandbox_image = "browserless/chrome"
# container_host = "127.0.0.1"   # or "host.docker.internal" in Docker

# Profile persistence
# persist_profile = true          # Keep cookies/auth across sessions
# profile_dir = "/custom/path"    # Custom profile location
```

---

### **Key Missing Features (Not Currently Configurable)**

Based on the config schema and code review:

1. **No per-domain profile isolation** - Single profile for all domains
2. **No cookie restrictions per action** - Can only export/import all or none
3. **No proxy configuration** - No per-request or global proxy settings
4. **No request interception** - Can't block/modify network requests
5. **No geolocation/timezone override** - Uses host system defaults
6. **No viewport profiles** - Only single configurable viewport size
7. **No authentication presets** - No built-in auth profiles or credential management
8. **No screenshot format options** - Only PNG base64 output

## Comprehensive Findings: Browser Profiles, Cookies, Session Creation, and Live Viewing in Moltis

### 1. Browser Profiles

**Location:** `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/browser/src/types.rs` lines 570-698

**Current Implementation:**
- **Profile Persistence:** Controlled by two BrowserConfig fields:
  - `persist_profile: bool` (default `true`) — enables profile persistence globally
  - `profile_dir: Option<String>` — optional custom profile directory path

- **Profile Directory Resolution:**
  - Method: `resolved_profile_dir()` (types.rs:663-671) returns `Some(path)` or `None`:
    - If `profile_dir` is set explicitly: uses that path
    - Else if `persist_profile` is true: uses `~/.moltis/browser/profile/` (moltis_config::data_dir)
    - Else: returns `None` (ephemeral/no persistence)

- **Profile Sharing Strategy:**
  - **Host Mode:** All agent sessions share a single host browser profile at `~/.moltis/browser/profile/`
  - **Sandbox Mode:** Per-session profile isolation via `sandbox_profile_dir()` (pool.rs:716-720)
    - Path structure: `{profile_root}/sandbox/{sanitized_session_id}/`
    - Sanitization prevents directory traversal (pool.rs:699-713)
  - **No per-agent isolation** — all agents in the same Moltis instance share profiles unless running in sandboxed containers

**Key Findings:**
- Profiles persist across sessions by default
- Multiple sessions can coexist and share browser state (cookies, cached data, etc.)
- Sandbox mode provides session-level isolation; host mode does not

---

### 2. Cookie Management

**Location:** `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/browser/src/types.rs` lines 126-137 and manager.rs lines 916-1026

**Cookie Export (manager.rs:917-973):**
- Requires existing session_id (required action)
- Uses CDP `Network.getCookies()` params with optional URL filtering
- Filter by domain: `GetCookiesParams { urls: Some(vec![current_url]) }`
- Returns vector of `ExportedCookie` with all metadata:
  - name, value, domain, path, secure, http_only, expires, same_site, size
- Can filter by domain parameter (optional)
- Output: JSON serialization compatible with clipboard export (page-browser.js:128-144)

**Cookie Import (manager.rs:976-1026):**
- BrowserAction variant `ImportCookies { cookies: Vec<CookieParam> }` (types.rs:134-137)
- CookieParam fields:
  - name, value (required)
  - domain, path (optional)
  - secure, http_only, expires, same_site (optional)
- Uses CDP `Network.setCookie()` for each cookie
- Supports timestamp-based expiry via `TimeSinceEpoch`
- SameSite enforcement: strict/lax/none

**No Import/Export API Endpoints:**
- No REST endpoint for bulk cookie export/import to disk
- UI can only copy cookies to clipboard (page-browser.js:136)
- Users must manually manage JSON or use browser DevTools

**Sharing Model:**
- Cookies are part of the browser profile
- Shared across all sessions using the same profile directory
- No per-session cookie isolation in host mode

---

### 3. Session Creation from UI

**Location:** `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/web/src/assets/js/page-browser.js` and crates/web/src/api.rs

**Session Lifecycle (page-browser.js):**

**List Sessions (lines 37-50):**
- Endpoint: `GET /api/browser/sessions`
- Polls every 5 seconds (line 230)
- Returns: `{ sessions: [ { session_id, url, age_secs, idle_secs, sandboxed, screencasting } ] }`
- Shows active browser sessions created by agents

**Create Session Implicitly:**
- No explicit "create new session" button in UI
- Sessions are created on-demand when agents request browser actions
- **Sessions appear in the list only after an agent initiates a browser action**
- First action (navigate, screenshot, etc.) auto-creates session via `pool.get_or_create()`

**Session Navigation from UI (lines 146-158):**
- User can navigate an existing session: `POST /api/browser/action` with `action: "navigate", url: "..."`
- Requires selecting a session first (no session creation from nav bar)
- Updates session metadata

**Cookie Export (lines 128-144):**
- One-click export: `POST /api/browser/action` with `action: "export_cookies"`
- Copies JSON to clipboard automatically
- No UI for cookie import

**Session Closing (lines 109-126):**
- `POST /api/browser/action` with `action: "close"`
- Removes from active list
- Stops screencast if active

**Limitations:**
- Cannot create browser sessions from UI directly
- Cannot import cookies from UI
- Sessions are read-only until a screencast starts (no direct navigation without viewing first technically, but code allows it)

---

### 4. Live Viewing / Screencast Architecture

**Location:** `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/browser/src/screencast.rs` and page-browser.js lines 52-107

**Screencast Registry (screencast.rs:71-193):**
- Central registry mapping session_id → active screencast
- Implements broadcast pattern for multi-subscriber support
- **Key Methods:**
  - `start()` (lines 97-150): Initiates CDP screencast, creates broadcast channel
    - Returns `broadcast::Receiver<ScreencastFrame>` for new subscribers
    - If already active, returns new subscriber to existing stream
  - `subscribe()` (lines 170-176): Late subscriber to existing screencast
  - `stop()` (lines 153-167): Stops CDP stream and aborts relay task
  - `active_sessions()` (lines 184-186): Lists all active screencast session IDs

**Frame Relay Background Task (screencast.rs:195-244):**
- Spawned on `start_screencast` action
- Listens to CDP `Page.screencastFrame` events
- Acknowledges frames with `ScreencastFrameAckParams` (line 215)
- Forwards base64 JPEG data to broadcast channel
- Detects lagged subscribers, logs and continues
- Terminates on stream end or channel drop

**ScreencastFrame Structure (screencast.rs:24-47):**
```
{
  session_id: String,
  data: String,           // base64-encoded JPEG
  metadata: {
    offset_top, page_scale_factor, device_width, device_height,
    scroll_offset_x, scroll_offset_y, timestamp
  },
  sequence: u64
}
```

**WebSocket Event Broadcasting (web/api.rs:1155-1185):**
- `api_browser_action_handler()` detects `start_screencast` action
- Spawns `spawn_screencast_relay()` task
- Subscribes to screencast frames and broadcasts as `browser.screencast.frame` event
- **Options:** `drop_if_slow: true` — skips frames for slow clients to prevent memory buildup
- All WebSocket clients receive frames via event bus

**UI Frame Display & Input (page-browser.js:337-410):**
- **Canvas Rendering:**
  - Decodes base64 JPEG frame data on arrival (line 357)
  - Draws to canvas with device dimensions (lines 349-356)
  - Maintains aspect ratio via CSS (line 407)

- **Mouse Input Relay (lines 162-201):**
  - Listens to canvas mousedown/mouseup/mousemove
  - Translates canvas coordinates to browser viewport using frame metadata:
    - scaleX = device_width / canvas_width
    - scaleY = device_height / canvas_height
  - Sends async (fire-and-forget) mouse_input CDP commands
  - Buttons: left/middle/right, click_count from e.detail

- **Keyboard Input Relay (lines 203-223):**
  - Canvas must have focus (tabindex="0", line 373)
  - Sends keyboard_input CDP commands with:
    - event_type: keyDown/keyUp
    - key, code, text fields
    - modifiers bitmask: Alt=1, Ctrl=2, Meta=4, Shift=8

**Multi-Viewer Support:**
- **Same-session multiple viewers:** Broadcast channel allows N subscribers
- **Different sessions:** Each session has independent screencast + relay task
- **No conflict:** Each viewer gets independent renderer on canvas; backend broadcasts to all

**Viewer Control During Agent Activity:**
- **No mutual exclusion:** Agent and UI can both interact with same session
- **Race conditions possible:** Both may send CDP commands simultaneously
- **Pool locking:** Session's `Arc<Mutex<BrowserInstance>>` ensures sequential page access
  - One action waits for previous to complete (mutex + async)
  - CDP itself handles concurrent requests via WebSocket protocol

---

### 5. Pool / Session Architecture

**Location:** `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno/crates/browser/src/pool.rs` lines 70-93

**Instance Management:**
```rust
struct BrowserInstance {
    browser: Browser,
    pages: HashMap<String, Page>,      // pages["main"] = primary page
    last_used: Instant,
    created_at: Instant,               // TTL: 30 min max (line 27)
    sandboxed: bool,
    container: Option<BrowserContainer>,
}

pub struct BrowserPool {
    config: BrowserConfig,
    instances: RwLock<HashMap<String, Arc<Mutex<BrowserInstance>>>>,
    active_count: AtomicUsize,
}
```

**Session Locking (pool.rs:195-240):**
- **Read lock on HashMap** to get instance reference
- **Mutex lock on instance** to access pages:
  ```rust
  let mut inst = instance.lock().await;  // Line 200
  inst.last_used = Instant::now();       // Update usage
  if let Some(page) = inst.pages.get("main") {
    return Ok(page.clone());
  }
  ```
- **Page is cloneable** (Chromium CDP Page handle) — multiple holders possible
- **Per-instance serialization:** Only one action at a time can lock the instance

**Multi-Consumer Implications:**
- Agent and UI both call `pool.get_page()` → both acquire same Mutex
- Concurrent CDP commands on same page allowed (Chromium handles async)
- But instance state updates (last_used, created_at) serialized
- **No true concurrent viewing + control** — only apparent concurrency via async

**Session Lifecycle:**
- **Create:** `pool.get_or_create(session_id, sandbox, browser_pref)` (line 111)
  - If session_id exists: return it
  - Else: generate new UUID-like session_id (pool.rs:691-695)
  - Launch browser (host or sandboxed)
  - Store in instances HashMap under session_id
- **Reuse:** Existing session_id returns same instance
- **Idle Timeout:** `cleanup_idle()` removes sessions idle > config.idle_timeout_secs (default 300s) (line 273)
- **Hard TTL:** Instances over MAX_BROWSER_INSTANCE_LIFETIME (30 min) forced close (lines 284-285)
- **Close:** `close_session(session_id)` removes instance, closes browser process

**Memory Management:**
- `memory_limit_percent` (default 90) blocks new instances if system usage exceeds threshold
- `max_instances` (default 0 = unlimited) hard cap if set
- Cleanup attempts before refusing new instances
- Low-memory Chrome flags auto-injected if system RAM < threshold (pool.rs:624-639)

**Profile Handling in Pool:**
- **Host mode:** Uses global `~/.moltis/browser/profile/`
- **Sandbox mode:** Per-session profile via `sandbox_profile_dir()` (line 390)
  - Directory created world-writable (0o777) for container uid mapping (line 427)
  - Profile data isolated per session but shared if same session reused

---

## Summary: Current Architecture

| Aspect | Current State |
|--------|--------------|
| **Profile Sharing** | Global for host mode; per-session for sandbox |
| **Cookie Isolation** | None in host mode; per-session in sandbox via profile |
| **Session Creation** | Implicit on first agent action; no UI button |
| **Cookie Management** | Export via CDP; import via CDP; clipboard export only |
| **Live Viewing** | Broadcast-based; multi-viewer per session possible |
| **Input Relay** | Mouse/keyboard sent async; no strict mutual exclusion with agent |
| **Pool Locking** | Mutex per instance; serialized page operations |
| **Session Reuse** | By session_id; persists for 30 min or until idle timeout |

Here's the current state after investigating:

**Fixed so far (this branch):**
1. **Null-stripping** — LLMs sending `null` for optional fields no longer crashes deserialization
2. **Shared manager** — `BrowserTool` and `RealBrowserService` now share one `BrowserManager`, so agent-created sessions appear in the UI

**What's already built:**
- Live viewing via CDP screencast with multi-subscriber broadcast
- Mouse/keyboard relay from UI canvas to browser session
- Cookie export/import via CDP
- Profile persistence enabled by default (`~/.moltis/browser/profile/`)

**What's still missing for your vision:**

| Feature | Status |
|---------|--------|
| See agent sessions in UI | **Fixed** (shared manager) |
| Create new session from UI | Missing — needs "New Session" button calling navigate API |
| Login manually (LinkedIn) via UI | Works once session exists (screencast + input relay) |
| Agent reuses cookies from manual login | Works — shared profile dir persists cookies |
| Per-agent browser profiles | Not built — currently single global profile in host mode |
| Peek session while agent controls it | Works — broadcast screencast, no mutual exclusion |

The biggest remaining gap is the **"Create Session" button** in the UI. The backend already supports it — sending a navigate action with no `session_id` to `/api/browser/action` creates a new session. The UI just doesn't expose it.

Want me to:
1. Add the "Create Session" UI button and commit everything together?
2. Or commit the two fixes first and plan the broader profile-per-agent architecture separately?

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(browser): share manager between tool and UI, add new session button

Three fixes for the browser viewing UI:

1. Strip explicit null values from LLM tool call params before
   deserialization — serde(default) only handles missing keys, not
   null values, causing "invalid type: null, expected u64" errors.

2. Share a single BrowserManager between BrowserTool and
   RealBrowserService via Arc<OnceCell>. Previously each had its own
   manager, so sessions created by agents were invisible to the UI.

3. Add "New Session" button to the browser page so users can create
   sessions directly from the UI (navigate to about:blank + auto-start
   screencast). Agents share the same cookie profile.
EOF
)"

content.text:
Pushed. Here's what this commit fixes:

1. **Null-stripping** — LLMs sending `null` for optional fields (like `timeout_ms`) no longer crash with "invalid type: null, expected u64"
2. **Shared manager** — `BrowserTool` and `RealBrowserService` now share one `BrowserManager` via `Arc<OnceCell>`, so agent-created sessions show up in the UI
3. **"New Session" button** — users can create browser sessions directly from the UI, auto-starts screencast for immediate interaction

content.text:
Pushed. Now let me address both issues — "New Session does nothing" and button styling in the session cards.

First, the "New Session" issue. The `createSession()` calls `/api/browser/action` which goes through `RealBrowserService::request()`. But `request()` deserializes directly into `BrowserRequest` — it doesn't strip nulls like the tool does. More importantly, the JS doesn't send a `session_id` at all (which is correct), but the service's `request()` method may not handle session creation through navigate properly since it doesn't go through the pool's get_or_create flow the same way. Let me trace the service path:

The service calls `self.manager().await` which initializes the OnceCell if needed — this is correct. But wait, since we're sharing the cell with the tool now, there could be a config mismatch. The service's `manager()` initializer uses `self.config`, but with `with_shared_manager` we passed `BrowserConfig::from(config)` without setting `container_prefix`. Let me check:
Let me check the API handler to understand why "New Session" does nothing:

content.text:
There it is — `about:blank` has scheme `about`, which is rejected by `validate_url()` (only `http`/`https` allowed). The "New Session" silently fails because the error is caught and shown as a toast, but the user might not notice it.

I need to either allow `about:blank` or navigate to a real URL. The simplest fix: create the session without navigating anywhere, or use a data URL. Actually, looking at the pool, `get_or_create` creates a session and opens a browser — the page starts at about:blank by default. So I need a way to just create a session without navigating.

Let me check if there's a "noop" action or if I should just skip the URL validation for about:blank:

content.text:
Pushed. Now for the "Waiting for first frame..." issue — the screencast starts but no frames arrive for an `about:blank` page. This makes sense: CDP screencast only sends frames when the page has visual content to render. A blank page may not trigger any frame events.

Let me check how the screencast works with blank pages:

From the logs, the issue is clear: `about:blank` doesn't generate screencast frames. The screencast works once the user navigates to a real URL. The fix: don't auto-start screencast on blank sessions — just select the session and show the navigate bar.

Line 98: `request.sandbox.unwrap_or(false)` — defaults to host mode. The UI doesn't send `sandbox: true`, so new sessions from the UI always run in host mode.

The `BrowserTool` injects sandbox based on the session's sandbox router, but the service has no such context. I need to add a default sandbox mode to the service. Let me check the `BrowserService` trait:
